review:
Com mais de 300 horas, a conclusão é que a Larian entregou o que deveria ser o padrão da indústria. O jogo é simplesmente perfeito e, mesmo depois de fechar várias vezes, cada nova run me faz descobrir coisas que eu nunca tinha visto.

A melhor mecânica de longe é a rolagem de dados para absolutamente tudo (diálogo, ações, combate, cálculo de dano). A imersão é tão bem feita que a sensação é de estar mestrando o seu próprio universo.

Joguei campanhas inteiras focado nos casters: Warlock, Wizard e Sorcerer. A subclasse Shadow Sorcerer foi de longe a minha favorita.

Aprofundei muito na história dos companions. Fiz questão de manter a classe padrão de cada um e montar builds que realmente faziam sentido com a lore. O nível de escrita não deixa ninguém ser genérico. Cada um tem uma personalidade marcante, com medos, gostos e humores completamente diferentes.

E o principal: tudo importa. Qualquer atitude com os NPCs tem peso e altera o decorrer da história de forma lógica. O roteiro é completamente amarrado, sem nenhuma ponta solta.

Mas foda-se toda essa complexidade. O verdadeiro ápice desse jogo é ignorar o mundo acabando pra adotar um cachorro e um urso-coruja e ficar fazendo carinho neles no acampamento.

Uma obra-prima.
10/10

review_english: With over 300 hours, the bottom line is that Larian has delivered what should be the industry standard. The game is simply perfect and, even after closing it several times, each new run makes me discover things I had never seen before. The best mechanic by far is the dice roll for absolutely everything (dialogue, actions, combat, damage calculation). The immersion is so well done that it feels like mastering your own universe. I played entire campaigns focused on Warlock, Wizard and Sorcerer spellcasters. The Shadow Sorcerer subclass was by far my favorite. I delved a lot deeper into the story of the companions. I made a point of maintaining each person's default class and putting together builds that really made sense with the lore. The level of writing prevents anyone from being generic. Each one has a striking personality, with completely different fears, tastes and moods. And most importantly, everything matters. Any attitude towards NPCs has weight and changes the course of the story in a logical way. The script is completely tied together, with no loose ends. But fuck all that complexity. The real highlight of this game is ignoring the world ending to adopt a dog and an owlbear and pet them at the camp. A masterpiece. 10 10

review:
Очень понравилась игра. Играл в паре с подругой. Возможность играть по сети для меня большой плюс. Обычно я забрасываю длинные игры больше 30ч, но вместе наиграли уже больше 60 и собираемся продолжать.

До BG3 не особо играл в CRPG, так что сравнить с другими представителями жанра не могу, но в игре огромное множество возможностей как в бою, так и разного рода социальных взаимодействиях с NPC. Порой возможности во взаимодействии с игровым миром действительно могут удивить.

Сложно найти игру, которая может соперничать с BG3 в плане количества контента и реиграбельности. С другой стороны, вряд ли бы я получил столько же удовольствия играя один, а не в коопе.

review_english: I really liked the game. Played with a friend. The ability to play online is a big plus for me. I usually abandon long games that last more than 30 hours, but together we have already played more than 60 and are going to continue. Before BG3, I didn’t play much CRPG, so I can’t compare it with other representatives of the genre, but the game has a huge number of possibilities both in combat and various kinds of social interactions with NPCs. Sometimes the possibilities in interacting with the game world can really surprise you. It's hard to find a game that can rival BG3 in terms of content and replay value. On the other hand, it’s unlikely that I would have had as much fun playing alone rather than in co-op.

review:
Baldur's Gate 3 não é apenas um RPG. É provavelmente uma das experiências mais completas e imersivas já feitas no gênero.

A liberdade que o jogo entrega é absurda. Quase toda situação pode ser resolvida de maneiras diferentes: diálogo, furtividade, manipulação, magia, combate ou simplesmente fazendo alguma ideia completamente maluca funcionar. O jogo realmente reage às suas escolhas e isso faz cada campanha parecer única.

Os companheiros são outro nível. Cada personagem tem personalidade, conflitos próprios e desenvolvimento extremamente bem escrito. Em muitos momentos eles parecem mais “reais” do que personagens de outros RPGs modernos. Shadowheart, Astarion, Karlach e Gale facilmente entram para o hall dos melhores companions já feitos.

O combate por turnos pode assustar no começo, principalmente para quem nunca jogou D&D, mas depois que você entende as mecânicas, o jogo vira um playground estratégico gigantesco. Posicionamento, terreno, fogo, empurrões, magia e criatividade importam mais do que simplesmente ter números altos.

Visualmente o jogo também impressiona muito. As animações faciais, a direção artística e a ambientação fazem o mundo parecer vivo o tempo inteiro.

Mas o maior mérito do jogo é algo raro hoje em dia: ele respeita o jogador. Não tenta te apressar, não te trata como incapaz e não segura sua mão o tempo inteiro. Você experimenta, falha, cria histórias próprias e descobre consequências naturalmente.

Pontos negativos?
O Ato 3 pode ficar um pouco pesado e confuso às vezes, o inventário é meio bagunçado e a curva de aprendizado inicial pode afastar alguns jogadores. Ainda assim, nada disso chega perto de diminuir o que esse jogo entrega.

Depois de jogar Baldur’s Gate 3, muitos RPGs parecem “menos vivos”.

Nota: 10/10.
review_clean: Baldur's Gate 3 não é apenas um RPG. É provavelmente uma das experiências mais completas e imersivas já feitas no gênero. A liberdade que o jogo entrega é absurda. Quase toda situação pode ser resolvida de maneiras diferentes diálogo, furtividade, manipulação, magia, combate ou simplesmente fazendo alguma ideia completamente maluca funcionar. O jogo realmente reage às suas escolhas e isso faz cada campanha parecer única. Os companheiros são outro nível. Cada personagem tem personalidade, conflitos próprios e desenvolvimento extremamente bem escrito. Em muitos momentos eles parecem mais reais do que personagens de outros RPGs modernos. Shadowheart, Astarion, Karlach e Gale facilmente entram para o hall dos melhores companions já feitos. O combate por turnos pode assustar no começo, principalmente para quem nunca jogou D D, mas depois que você entende as mecânicas, o jogo vira um playground estratégico gigantesco. Posicionamento, terreno, fogo, empurrões, magia e criatividade importam mais do que simplesmente ter números altos. Visualmente o jogo também impressiona muito. As animações faciais, a direção artística e a ambientação fazem o mundo parecer vivo o tempo inteiro. Mas o maior mérito do jogo é algo raro hoje em dia ele respeita o jogador. Não tenta te apressar, não te trata como incapaz e não segura sua mão o tempo inteiro. Você experimenta, falha, cria histórias próprias e descobre consequências naturalmente. Pontos negativos? review_english: Baldur's Gate 3 isn't just an RPG. It's probably one of the most complete and immersive experiences ever made in the genre. The freedom that the game offers is absurd. Almost every situation can be resolved in different ways through dialogue, stealth, manipulation, magic, combat or simply making some completely crazy idea work. The game really reacts to your choices and this makes each campaign feel unique. The companions are another level. Each character has their own personality, conflicts and extremely well-written development. In many moments they seem more real than characters from other modern RPGs. Shadowheart, Astarion, Karlach and Gale easily join the ranks of the best companions ever made. The turn-based combat can be scary at first, especially for those who have never played D D, but once you understand the mechanics, the game becomes a gigantic strategic playground. Positioning, terrain, fire, pushing, magic and creativity matter more than simply having high numbers. Visually, the game is also very impressive. The facial animations, artistic direction and setting make the world feel alive all the time. But the game's greatest merit is something rare nowadays: it respects the player. He doesn't try to rush you, he doesn't treat you as incapable and he doesn't hold your hand the whole time. You experiment, fail, create your own stories and discover consequences naturally. Negative points? Act 3 can get a little overwhelming and confusing at times, the inventory is a bit messy, and the initial learning curve may put some players off. Still, none of this comes close to detracting from what this game delivers. After playing Baldur's Gate 3, many RPGs feel less alive. Rating 10 10.

review:
Every ounce of praise and hype this game has received over the past 3 years have been warranted. One of the greatest RPGs ever crafted. HOWEVER, if you're planning to start a group run, there are a few caveats to be aware of:

- Dialogue is not designed around a group of 2+ custom characters, resulting in some awkward immersion-shaking dialogue scenes and story moments

- As far as we could tell, there is no way in the base game to accommodate for NPC companions if you have a full set of 4 people, resulting in missing out on key side quests and necessary character inclusions for main quests (which will cause some of your companions to completely turn on you and leave in a few cases)

- Only 1 player can really participate in dialogue, with the others only able to give suggestions. Very hard to tell which lines of dialogue are shared between all characters and which ones are only recognized by NPCs on an individual level

Co-op could have been much better implemented, but I fully understand how difficult it probably was to even get it in its current state without compromising the single-player or putting in a few extra million dollars for tons of extra dialogue. Therefore I absolutely still give it a high rating. Just figured it would be best to warn those who are buying it solely for co-op of the compromises.

review:
I genuinely adore this game, it is one of the best games I've ever played. The game mechanics are simple and easy to learn, the graphics are outstanding and the storyline is immersive and highly interactive, every decision you make matters! One of the best aspect for me is the turn based fighting and as someone with a very slow reaction speed, it gives me time to really think through my actions and strategize rather than key smashing till my opponent's dead. The incorporation of D&D mechanics is also really fun because sometimes no amount of strategizing can hold up against the dice rolls. Also be prepared to spend plenty of time in this game. 132 hours and I'm still in Act 1 with 0 regrets.

All in all, it is a brilliant fantasy RPG, I HIGHLY recommend!

review:
I enjoy a wide variety of videogames, but there's something about CRPGs that will always be special to me. I still remember unwrapping my copy of STAR WARS Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords, installing it on my dad's old Windows XP box computer (yes, the grey one with a monitor as big as your head), and being transported into a new world of stories and gameplay. It probably ran at a crisp 15-20 frames per second and crashed constantly, but I didn't care. I was immersed in my character, forced to make choices that would alter the fate of party members I had painstakingly nurtured relationships with. 

Much, much further down the line in life, I become engrossed in Divinity: Original Sin II, a Kickstarted game from a relatively small indie studio. It consumed my every waking thought until the credits finally rolled. I of course enjoy many other CRPGs, but something about DOS 2 really gave me a taste of the obsession and immersion I had felt when I was younger. However, I wasn't sure I would experience that same feeling again any time soon.

When Baldur's Gate 3 launched out of early access and into full release, I took off a whole week and a half from my job to stay at home and experience it uninterrupted. It was the best PTO I've ever spent. I remember listening to Alfira's song in the Emerald Grove for the first time, a moment I could have easily missed depending on my dialogue options, and I recall being struck by how truly special this game was. I can't fault any other studios for not having the same level of production value as Larian, but the studio has a unique talent for writing character that really make you care so much about them, feel connected to them, relate to their struggles and empathize with them, motion captured cutscenes or no. 

After 120 hours of gameplay, I watched the credits roll. Unlike many other I've seen online, I couldn't bring myself to dive right back into the game. I needed a break. However, I have returned several times and completed many other playthroughs (though I still have not completed a Dark Urge playthrough, something I am currently working on). In fact, I completed Honor mode in 2024, and am only a handful of achievements away from 100%, something I'd never set out to do for any videogame I have played before. 

I also have to praise Larian for all the years they spent improving and expanding the game from 2023-2025 (for free, I might add).

Baldur's Gate 3 became a gateway for me: I found myself engrossed in D&D actual plays, learning tabletop systems, and even running some games of my own. Honestly, in that way, you could say this game changed my life, as TTRPGs and collaborative storytelling has become a large part of my life. 

I know this is a very long, rambling, poorly formatted review from off the top of my head, but if I had to sum up: The game is good, I think everyone should try it at least once.

And Thank You Larian, for creating a game that made me feel so in touch with my inner child again, and opening a new doorway for so many new fans to the genre.

review:
写于2026年完整体验完一周目联机游戏之后
我时常在想，要说史上最伟大的游戏可能要结合时代因素，很难抛开时之笛野吹俄罗斯方块大菠萝2这些游戏，那么说史上最牛逼的游戏应该就是博德之门3了。实际上在2023年我感情上是更喜欢王泪的，我觉得王泪的建造系统是比野吹”化学引擎“更牛逼的创新，但是2023年真玩游戏的人不会对博德之门3拿到年度游戏有任何争议。
时至今日，bg3依然以令人咋舌的线性游戏容量、网状叙事结构、超复杂多样的角色构筑养成、极强的拉瑞安拿手的剧情自由度和各种剧情走向兜底机制、高剧情自由度和一些没有卵用的文本带来的极大角色代入感、放在3年后也不算落伍的画面和优化、几首脍炙人口的小曲、完全不同的多周目剧情体验和联机体验，成为我心目中最牛逼的电子游戏。（当然，这也是站在DND这个巨人的肩膀上获得的成就。但是bg3在传统DND的基础上做出的类似简化战斗掷骰流程之类的努力也是起到了不可忽视的润滑游戏体验的作用的）

缺点：即使是更新了数个补丁的三年后的现在，bg3仍然有不少bug……不过大多数都可以靠重启游戏解决

review_english: Written in 2026 after fully experiencing a week of online games, I often think about it. To say that the greatest game in history may be based on the factors of the times, it is difficult to put aside games such as Ocarina of Time Nobuki Tetris Big Pineapple 2. Then the most awesome game in history should be Baldur's Gate 3. In fact, in 2023, I prefer Wang Lei emotionally. I think Wang Lei's construction system is a more awesome innovation than Nobuki Chemical Engine. But those who actually play games in 2023 will not have any controversy about Baldur's Gate 3 winning the Game of the Year. To this day, bg3 still has an astonishing linear game capacity, a network narrative structure, and ultra-complex and diverse character building and development. Larian is good at plot freedom and various plot mechanisms to avoid the plot. The high plot freedom and some useless texts bring a great sense of character substitution. The graphics and optimization are not outdated even after 3 years. A few popular ditties, a completely different multi-story plot experience and online experience, it has become the most awesome video game in my mind. Of course, this is also an achievement achieved by standing on the shoulders of the giant DND. However, bg3's efforts on the basis of traditional DND, such as simplifying the combat dice process, have also played a role in lubricating the game experience that cannot be ignored. Disadvantages Even now, three years after updating several patches, bg3 still has many bugs, but most of them can be solved by restarting the game.

review:
This game has genuinely become my favourite game so quickly, so much so that when I finally got my own pc (I was borrowing my partners before) this was the first game I purchased.

It is incredibly story-rich and has so many different choices, approaches etc. that is allows for so much replayability. You can get incredibly creative in the way you approach different interactions, quests and so on. Furthermore the characters are so well written, they all have inticing backstories and unique personalities, and they make it so easy to get emotionally invested. It is the first videogame that has genuinely made me cry. As someone who's a massive DnD nerd, I was also especially impressed with how well they translated so many elements of DnD into a videogame format. 

I really couldn't reccommend it enough. Whether you're the type of player who just wants to experience a great story and have fun romancing a bunch of interesting characters, or you're into DnD and want a videogame that embodies that, or you're someone who's super into min-maxxing a character and finding new fun ways to deal with combat scenarios, or you just want to gather a group of friends and ♥♥♥♥ around in a big open world full of adventure, BG3 will have something for you.

review:
It can make you laugh. It can make you cry. It can make your real world disappear as you explore the wonderful world of Faerûn.

The quality and attention to details in this game is stunning. You are automatically immersed into this world because of rich characters with full voice acting and a world with tons of lore. The combat system feels very smooth and allows you to do (almost) anything you have in mind.

Allowing for types of spells that have often been left out in other games in this genre, like Frineds, Detect Thoughts and Speak with Dead makes a spellcaster usable for other things than blasting your enemies. Most D&D players know that Guidance is the best spell in 5th edition. Larian knew that as well <3.

There are so many good things to say about this game but I will stop myself here and just say one more thing. Play this game if you like the genre, it will not disappoint.

review:
When I first heard about Baldur's Gate 3, it was while I was halfway through playing Divinity Original Sin 2 in 2023. Some friends and I had been going through DoS2 separately and they were expressing great excitement towards the game and it's imminent release. Of course I'd never heard about it but upon learning it was being created by the same developers responsible for the game I was currently enjoying (and the front runner for my favorite gaming experience so far that year), I looked it up and the rest is history.

I just finished my second full play through of the game, this time as a co-op play through with two friends. We started in April of 2025 and finished Mid-May 2026. What a journey it was. It honestly feels bittersweet that I get my Monday evenings back now because it means I've finished the grand adventure that is Baldur's Gate 3. And this time, likely for a long long time. 

There are games with better graphics, better animations, combat, mechanics, and possibly even better story. But the journey one goes on from waking up in the Nautiloid ship to finally arriving in Baldur's Gate proper is truly sublime. Larian crafted a masterpiece generational game that will be talked about like all the greats that came before it. Play it. Experience it. Enjoy it.

review:
Obligatory "this game is so boring" comment despite having 1000+ hours. 

Anyway, this game is genuinely really well made. There are some downsides such as obviously cut content especially when it comes to Act 3/the end of the game and some of the companions (Wyll and Karlach especially), but overall the game is very enjoyable. There is a ton of content to explore, and I've found new stuff in every playthrough (and I thought I was thorough in my first 3 playthroughs lol). 

The built in mod manager makes modding the game incredibly easy even for technological nincompoops like me. I'll also admit that I'm also not usually the smartest or most motivated when it comes to building characters in games like this, but it's relatively easy to understand in BG3 (though I can't be sure if that's due to the DND building or not as I haven't had the change to play DND irl).

I've been playing this game since it came out, and I've had about 5 full playthroughs (you could obviously have far more than me in 1000+ hours, but I love Acts 1 & 2, so I always restart to replay them). If I had to explain why I don't like Act 3 as much without spoilers, I'd say it's because it's so overwhelming. There's not any sort of "recommended" order to do things in (which is understandable because it's supposed to be up to you), but I found Acts 1 & 2 much easier to follow without feeling like I had to do 10 things at once.

The companions and NPCs are well made characters for the most part (see my previous comment on cut content), and I love the reoccurring NPCs and their storylines. I think a lot of passion and community requests were put into many of the companions, but there is definitely a large gap between the content quantity of (for example) Shadowheart and Karlach or Astarion and Wyll. I am NOT saying it's bad that certain companions have a lot of content, and I adore all of the companions, but I just wish there were more to some of their personal stories and their romances. I will say that I highly enjoy the storylines of even the companions without as much content - just as much as I enjoy the longer content-having ones. However, there will always be a bit of a hole in their stories.

I've written quite a bit, and while I could talk about this game for ages, I'll stop here! Overall I'd say this is a good 8.5/10 game taking everything into account.
If you decide to play, have so much fun! It's really worth it! :)

review:
Amazing game, and amazingly it is totally playable on my system, which is far below the listed minimum for graphics. With FSR 2.2 on quality mode and textures on high/medium for terrain i get a shockingly good image at 1600*900 averaging somewhere around 20fps.
This is extremely volatile based on the area and circumstance however as i get 40fps some places and as low as barely 10 in the most intense of scenes. With the game having turn based combat however this is a perfectly acceptable tradeoff to have good image quality in cutscenes especially

review:
going into this game i didnt know what to expect, everyone kept hyping the freedom of choice and consequence in an attempt to sell me this game, however when i experienced this masterpiece of a game i found profoundly beautiful character writing and one of the best written plots in gaming.
dont get me wrong the freedom of choice enhances your experience but the main bread and butter of this game is exploration, character writing and nuance in choices. i dont know how larian managed to make such a massive game with so many choices without sacrificing any quality in writing, the combat or the graphics. everything is outstanding and now baldurs gate 3 has become my favorite game ever dethroning expedition 33. 
peak 10/10 would love to put a thousand more hours in this game.

review: This game genuinely saved my life. I was at such a low point in my life, and on a whim I decided to boot up this silly game; it was a great decision. As corny as it is, it really did help me get through a rough patch of my life, and when I thought things couldn't get better, I discovered the modding scene. Although I don't play this game as much anymore, since I got my life together and have a job, it still holds a place in my heart none-the-less. If you have the spare change, get this game! The community is also awesome, and is where I found solace in along with many new friends. The only issue being: the game is MASSIVE... which that is another reason I do not play it much! My pc is taken up by work files and the occasional co-op, so I advise having a bit of spare storage if you wanna indulge in this wonderful game.

Sen oyuna yüzlerce saat içerik dök. Her yan görevi incelikle işle, sıradan bir köy meselesi bile olsa arkasına saatlerce emek ver. Tüm bu görevler için ayrı müzik hazırla. Her karaktere geçmiş ver: nereden geldi, amacı ne, neden burada?
Sonra o karakterin geleceğini, oyuncunun istediği gibi şekillendirebilme imkanı sun.
Sonra lavuğun biri gelsin bu şaheseri 40 saatte bitirsin ve sen buna kızamazsın bile.
Çünkü bu lavuk parasını ödedi.

review_english: You put hundreds of hours of content into the game. Handle each side quest meticulously, putting hours of effort into it, even if it's an ordinary village issue. Prepare separate music for all these tasks. Give background to each character, where did he come from, what is his purpose, why is he here? Then offer the opportunity to shape the future of that character as the player wishes. Then some jerk comes and finishes this masterpiece in 40 hours and you can't even be angry about it. Because this fucker paid his dues.

review:
Как же я старался выдавить из себя что-то хорошее, но каждый блятский акт меня загонял в ещё большую фрустрацию и отторжение.
Когда-то давно я восхищался первым актом и надеялся на опыт Divinity в кубе, но по итогу чем дальше - тем больше понимаешь, какой же кал разработчики сделали в итоге, напихав сюда кучу ненужного хлама (во всех смыслах) и нагрузив это тонной дерьмовых механик.
Давайте сразу проясним, я обожаю игры этой студии, мне ♥♥♥♥♥♥ как неприятно писать такое, но я не могу врать себе чисто из-за разработчиков.
И да, я понимаю, что все мои претензии можно решить самостоятельно модами или читами, но ♥♥♥♥♥ мне это надо, если я хотел получить это в изначальной версии игры?
Да, я на этапе покупки понимал, что моё отвращение к кубикам, ну вот прям точно попортит отношение к игре, но не думал, что настолько... Потому что я готов идти искать читы, лишь бы отключит это дерьмо, ведь оно ♥♥♥♥♥ не нужно в компьютерной игре, вернее не нужно в таком виде, где каждые 2 минуты ты слышишь или видишь броски на всё подряд. Просто идёшь мимо какой-то хуеты? Бросок! Любое действие с противником в бою? Бросок! Основной сюжетный квест или очень важный выбор, ради которого ты максил одну характеристику с начала игры? Бросок! Критический, ♥♥♥♥♥, промах) Да ♥♥♥♥♥, сейвскаммим, это же ♥♥♥♥♥♥ интересно. Сложность эта система не даёт, время тратит, заёбывает постоянно, погружение руинит. Ок, пусть будет, но почему я не могу хотя бы скрыть это говно? Пусть комп высчитывает всё сам где-то за кадром (как в любой другой игре, охуеть, да?), зачем мне это наблюдать самому? И что, ♥♥♥♥, самое "интересное", у некоторых классов есть буквально иммунитет в некоторых действиях к этому говну. Тот же вор может прокачать себя настолько, что взлом ЛЮБОГО замка в игре будет для него всегда на 100% успешным.
Из кубиков вытекает и всратая боёвка, в которой мало того, что есть просто конченные противники, которых невозможно представить себе в DND, который может делать 5-6-7-да ♥♥♥♥♥, хоть 10 действий подряд в пошаговой игре, которые невосприимчивы ни к одному из имеющихся у тебя в пачке урону, так ещё и все бои проходят в стиле X-COM, где твои 4 додика, с любыми характеристиками, могут не попасть в крысу 1 лвла 2 хода подряд ничем, даже гранатой ♥♥♥♥♥)
Ну ладно, ♥♥♥ на кубики, наверняка тут квесты выше всяких похвал? Да, реально тут годные и интересные квесты, но... ты не пройдёшь их как хочешь, потому что смотри пункт с кубиками, потому что ты можешь даже не получить квест из-за провала где-то там когда-то проверки внимания. Я могу понять оправдание боёвки, мол крыса мелкая и она от гранат за камнем пряталась, но каким хуем мой ДЦПшник каждый раз проваливает абсолютно любую проверку на тех характеристиках, в которых он максимально хорош? Я пробовал "идти по течению", не использовать сейвы и просто забивать на переигрывание, даже если мне интересно что там могло бы быть. И чё? Ну я где-то половину квестов въебал просто, ну круто. 
Система отдыха... Ну это как кубики, только эту парашу даже непонятно для чего придумали. В конце первого акта у меня припасов на 100 "отдыхов" вперёд. В конце второго - максимальный уровень персонажа. Боёвка от наличия этой системы стала сложнее? Да нет, унылее стала. При отсутствии прогрессии из-за уровня и вместе с таким понятием как "ебейший дисбаланс шмоток", что мне дальше делать в игре? Ходить облизывать каждый угол Балдура ради чего? Ну только ради сюжета.
Сюжет... Да так ♥♥♥♥♥ на него, если честно. Ну я уже в середине игры понял какой будет финал, всё остальное - путь самурая к слезливо всратой концовке "убиваем мозг и все спасены" или "подчиняем мозг и мы топ 1 мразь", ну да, там есть ещё варианты, наверное, даже всю пачку порешать, но это же финал, ну поработит мой додик мир, ну покажут мне заставку, это даст как-то сильно порофлить под конец игры? Заставит Астариона выебать Лаэзель? Или наоборот? Нет ведь. Ну это же игра, причём ♥♥♥♥♥ не короткая, ну неужели не сам путь должен доставлять удовольствие, а не горение жопы от кубиков и нонстоп провалов, причём провалов не по вине твоего скилла или невнимательности, или даже QTE, а просто из-за нарочито всратого рандома.

Аж стул задымался, ♥♥♥♥♥♥.
Ну, я ещё вернусь в попытках пройти это в 4-ый раз, и наверняка так же сгорю ещё раз... Напомнило Notia прям. Выходит, не такая уж дерьмовая игры, а я, вообще-то, отрицательный отзыв писал. Да всё равно кусок говна. Сделайте уже Дивайнити ещё одну часть.

review:
I nearly didn’t play Baldur’s Gate 3.

Not because I don’t like RPGs - quite the opposite. Some of my favourite games of all time are RPGs. The reason I stayed away was because I bounced hard off Divinity: Original Sin 2. I know it’s a beloved game, but I found the combat slow, overwhelming and, if I’m honest, a bit boring. Every time someone recommended BG3, all I could think was:

“Isn’t that just Divinity combat again?”

I’m very glad I was wrong.

Baldur’s Gate 3 is quite possibly the best RPG I’ve ever played.

What sets it apart isn’t the graphics, the voice acting or the production value (all of which are exceptional). It’s the sheer amount of player agency. Ten different people can play this game and end up with ten genuinely different stories. Characters can live, die, betray you, be saved, be ignored or never even be met. Entire questlines can play out completely differently depending on your decisions.

My own playthrough ended with dead companions, unlikely alliances, a trip to Hell to rob a devil, and decisions I never would have predicted when I first woke up on a beach with a tadpole in my eye.

And here’s the thing I’d say to anyone who is on the fence because the game looks intimidating:

Give it a chance.

If character creation, classes, feats, spells and level-up screens look overwhelming, use a guide. Watch a video. Ask a friend. Use AI. Seriously. There is absolutely no shame in getting help understanding the systems. I did, and I’m convinced I enjoyed the game far more because of it.

Don’t let the complexity put you off experiencing one of the greatest RPGs ever made.

I came for the story.

I stayed for over 100 hours.

And when the credits rolled, I immediately started wondering what would have happened if I’d made different choices.

That’s probably the highest compliment I can give it.

Не понимаю я прикола этой игры. Так же как и Divinity Original Sin 1 и 2.
Игра - просто набор механик. Просто бегаешь, говоришь с npc, дерёшься. Но нет цельной картины сюжета, нет смысла во всём происходящем, просто бред какой-то в кат-сценах и диалогах.

review:
It’s no surprise that this game has won so many awards. I want to thank the developers for putting their soul into this project.

What I liked: the combat system, build variety, voice acting, graphics, environmental interactions, visual style, and memorable characters.

What I didn't like: the plot is dragged out and very simple, especially once the initial intrigue is over. I also disliked that there is no option to reject all factions and go against everyone.
The third act feels too long and overloaded, especially if you play the game without taking breaks. If you carefully explored everything earlier, by the time you reach Act 3, you just don't have the energy to push forward; it simply becomes boring.
Another issue is that even on the highest difficulty, the game becomes very easy at high levels. Neither boss fights nor crowds of hostile NPCs pose any challenge.
For me, the ideal ending was the end of Act 2. I think the game could have ended right there. After Act 2, I spent most of my time just testing different builds and combat tactics. Having tried everything, I stopped playing without ever finishing Act 3.

I hope the plot in Larian’s next game will be deep and unpredictable enough to keep players engaged until the end without getting boring. In any case, it's a great game, and I recommend everyone try it at least once.

Another issue is that even on the highest difficulty, the game becomes very easy at high levels. Neither boss fights nor crowds of hostile NPCs pose any challenge. For me, the ideal ending was the end of Act 2. I think the game could have ended right there. After Act 2, I spent most of my time just testing different builds and combat tactics. Having tried everything, I stopped playing without ever finishing Act 3. I hope the plot in Larian s next game will be deep and unpredictable enough to keep players engaged until the end without getting boring. In any case, it's a great game, and I recommend everyone try it at least once.

review:
Наиграв 200+ часов, могу составить какое-никакое мнение.

Сюжет неплохой, графика тоже понравилась — особенно после Divinity 2.
Но основной камень преткновения — геймплей.

Игра основана на настольной системе DnD 5-й редакции. Я раньше не играл в подобные проекты, поэтому поначалу не особо понимал, как всё работает.

Пошаговые бои интересные, особенно если использовать окружение, эликсиры, свитки — в общем, задействовать все механики игры на максимум.

Но вот кубики — это, на мой взгляд, полная чушь. Всё слишком сильно завязано на удаче.
На сложности “Тактика” невозможно просчитывать ходы на 100%, как, например, в шахматах, потому что существует так называемый “критический промах”. И неважно, сколько у тебя бонусов к проверке — ты всё равно можешь промахнуться или получить дебафф просто из-за неудачного броска. Из-за этого складывается ощущение, что игра — это просто казино.

Баги — отдельная тема. Несмотря на то, что игре уже два года, я ни в одной игре не встречал столько багов. Камера часто ведёт себя неадекватно, скачет, из-за чего происходят миссклики.

Система отдыха в лагере тоже вызывает вопросы. Ты бегаешь по сюжету, сражаешься, и тебе постоянно приходится возвращаться в лагерь, чтобы восстановить ресурсы. Это сильно ломает темп игры.
Гораздо удобнее было бы сделать кнопку отдыха, которая просто восстанавливает всё и списывает ресурсы, а при наличии важного сюжетного диалога автоматически переносит в лагерь.

Отдельно стоит упомянуть так называемый сейвскам. Можно сохраняться и загружаться прямо во время диалогов, чтобы рероллить кубики до нужного результата.
Также приходится читать практически все книги и записки — иначе в диалогах может не появиться нужный вариант, например, чтобы избежать неравного боя.

Головоломок в игре мало, и они слишком простые. Сложными они покажутся разве что ребёнку.
Оружия и брони тоже немного, а исследование мира поощряется слабо.

Ну и вишенка на торте — сильный дисбаланс между сложностями “Баланс” и “Тактика”. На “Балансе” местами становится скучно, а на “Тактике” встречаются неоправданно сложные боссы. Апостолу Миркула — отдельный привет.

В целом всё, что хотел сказать, сказал. Итоговая оценка — 7.5/10.

review_english: Having played 200 hours, I can form some opinion. The plot is not bad, I also liked the graphics, especially after Divinity 2. But the main stumbling block is the gameplay. The game is based on the DnD 5th edition tabletop system. I hadn’t played such projects before, so at first I didn’t really understand how everything worked. Turn-based battles are interesting, especially if you use the environment, elixirs, scrolls in general, and use all the game mechanics to the maximum. But the cubes, in my opinion, are complete nonsense. Everything depends too much on luck. On Tactics difficulty it is impossible to calculate moves by 100, as, for example, in chess, because there is a so-called critical miss. And no matter how many check bonuses you have, you can still miss or get a debuff simply because of a bad roll. Because of this, it feels like the game is just a casino. Bugs are a separate issue. Despite the fact that the game is already two years old, I have never encountered so many bugs in any game. The camera often behaves inappropriately, jumping around, which causes missclicks. The recreation system in the camp also raises questions. You run through the story, fight, and constantly have to return to the camp to restore resources. This greatly breaks the pace of the game. It would be much more convenient to make a rest button that simply restores everything and writes off resources, and if there is an important plot dialogue, it automatically transfers you to the camp. Separately, it is worth mentioning the so-called savescam. You can save and load directly during dialogues to reroll the cubes to the desired result. You also have to read almost all the books and notes, otherwise the desired option may not appear in the dialogues, for example, to avoid an unequal battle. There are few puzzles in the game, and they are too simple. Only a child will find them difficult. There are also few weapons and armor, and exploration of the world is poorly encouraged. Well, the cherry on the cake is a strong imbalance between the Balance and Tactics difficulties. In Balance it gets boring in places, and in Tactics you encounter unreasonably difficult bosses. Special greetings to Apostle Mirkul. In general, everything I wanted to say was said. Final score 7.5 10.

review:
Не буду говорить насчёт популярности и хайпа. Сразу к сути:
Мы играем в самый дорогой пример игры подобного жанра, сделанного от компании, которая на этом собаку съела. 
Геймплей — 10/10, кровь и кубы, что может быть лучше? Очень хорошо описывал это один ютубер: "Если вы в голове смогли логично объединить описание двух предметов/заклинаний/ситуаций в свою пользу — скорее всего оно так и сработает". Сжигайте всё, закидайте всех ядами, имея к нему невосприимчивость, используйте молнию на луже, которая за бой покрыла всё поле боя. + за поддержку модов
Графика и интерфейс — на моём ноутбуке (бюджетном, стоит отметить) всё пошло на средних настройках и я не жаловался. Интерфейс интуитивно понятен, хотя иногда может сыграть злую шутку при наведении на противника или попытке использовать навык при незакреплённой панели.
Сюжет и повествование — вот тут и стоит камень преткновения, который всё для меня изрядно портит: после второго акта игра будто бы слишком спешит. Спору нет, сюжет моё почтение, но как по мне большая часть антагонистов плоские их можно было бы раскрыть или дописать им историю хоть на целый акт. Но имеем, что есть.
Мультиплеер — то, что вы и ожидаете, рекомендую или же играть с 1 другом, чтобы взять 1 напарника каждому или найти 3 друзей и веселится, потому что тут ужасно проработана часть взаимодействия с напарниками при мультиплеере
Моя оценка: 8.5 из 10

review_english: I won’t talk about popularity and hype. Straight to the point We are playing the most expensive example of a game of this genre, made from a company that ate the dog at this. Gameplay 10 10, blood and cubes, what could be better? One YouTuber described this very well: “If in your head you were able to logically combine the description of two situation spell items in your favor, most likely it will work.” Burn everything, shower everyone with poison, being immune to it, use lightning on the puddle that covered the entire battlefield during the battle. for supporting mods Graphics and interface on my laptop (budget, it’s worth noting) everything worked on medium settings and I didn’t complain. The interface is intuitive, although sometimes it can play a cruel joke when aiming at an enemy or trying to use a skill when the panel is not fixed. The plot and narrative here is where the stumbling block stands, which pretty much spoils everything for me after the second act, the game seems to be in too much of a hurry. No doubt, I respect the plot, but as for me, most of the antagonists are flat; they could be revealed or a story could be added to them for at least a whole act. But we have what we have. Multiplayer is what you expect, I recommend either play with 1 friend to take 1 partner for each or find 3 friends and have fun, because the part of interaction with partners in multiplayer is terribly worked out here. My rating is 8.5 out of 10

review:
An sich ist es sicherlich ein gutes Spiel.

Aber die ganzen Hype Bewertungen von Gamestar und Co kann ich leider nicht nachvollziehen. Innerhalb 
der ersten 5 Spielstunden hatte ich schon 2 Bugs.

Die Dialoge zwischen dem Hauptchar und den Nebenchars beinhalten teilweise Fragen / Aussagen, zu Dingen die noch gar nicht passiert sind oder die der jeweilige Nebencharakter vielleicht gar nicht mitbekommen hat, weil er/sie im Lager war.


Das Game crashed regelmäßig zum Desktop, was dazu führt, dass ich meinen Spielstand verliere. Ständiges Schnellspeichern zerstört für mich ein bisschen das Gamegefühl.

Obwohl Deutschland wirklich hervorragende Synchronsprecher hat, ist keine deutsche Vertonung geplant.

Der "Verdorrte" ist einfach in meinem Lager aufgetaucht, ohne dass ich die dazugehörige Questline überhaupt angefangen hate. Erst später bin ich überhaupt auf seine Grabkammer gestoßen.

Wenn man das Game also nicht ganz in der Reihenfolge spielt, wie die Entwickler es ggfs vorgesehen habe, passieren komische Dinge.

review_english: It's certainly a good game in itself. But unfortunately I can't understand all the hype reviews from Gamestar and Co. Within the first 5 hours of play I already had 2 bugs. The dialogues between the main character and the supporting characters sometimes contain questions about things that haven't happened yet or that the respective supporting character may not have even noticed because he was in the camp. The game regularly crashes to the desktop, causing me to lose my save. For me, constant quick saving ruins the feeling of the game a bit. Although Germany has really excellent dubbing actors, there are no plans for a German dubbing. The "Withered One" simply appeared in my camp without me even starting the associated questline. It was only later that I even came across his burial chamber. So if you don't play the game in the order the developers intended, strange things happen.

review:
Верните богов!

Жизни Куриц Важны!

Свободу перемещения фамильярам!

Игра конечно великолепна, но для стольких наград слишком многое упущено.

ПыСы: дивиз Лариан для этой игры - для нас сложно, но мы скажем это фишка в угоду сюжета и свободы игроков!
Выбор Сильвануса - для нас сложно, выбор бога для не клирика - это опционально в ДнД5, поэтому вообще не будем делать, т.к. для нас сложно, выбор мировоззрения - для нас сложно, но мы выдадим это за фишку свободы выбора и т.д. и т.п.

ПыПыСы: понимаю что ПыСы написал как поклонник Забытых королевств, но и без этого есть претензии: покупал по предзаказу, но играть не стал, ждал патчей что бы не портить впечатление, в итоге так и откладывал 3 года, а недавно подумал ну теперь то всё должно быть отлично! За 10 часов столкнулся с просачиванием фамильяра в пещеру, и невозможность из неё выйти, оказывается у нас фишка такая, фамильяры не ходят через локации, обещал курице её спасти, но как оказалось это заранее заложенный обман, нет такого варианта, несколько раз выборы в диалогах вели не к тому что предполагалось на основе этого самого диалога, и наконец почитав обсуждения и форумы я понял что таких допущений очень много, и они мешают целиком погрузится в игру.

ПыСы дивиз Лариан для этой игры - для нас сложно, но мы скажем это фишка в угоду сюжета и свободы игроков! Выбор Сильвануса - для нас сложно, выбор бога для не клирика - это опционально в ДнД5, поэтому вообще не будем делать, т.к. для нас сложно, выбор мировоззрения - для нас сложно, но мы выдадим это за фишку свободы выбора и т.д. и т.п. ПыПыСы понимаю что ПыСы написал как поклонник Забытых королевств, но и без этого есть претензии покупал по предзаказу, но играть не стал, ждал патчей что бы не портить впечатление, в итоге так и откладывал 3 года, а недавно подумал ну теперь то всё должно быть отлично! За 10 часов столкнулся с просачиванием фамильяра в пещеру, и невозможность из неё выйти, оказывается у нас фишка такая, фамильяры не ходят через локации, обещал курице её спасти, но как оказалось это заранее заложенный обман, нет такого варианта, несколько раз выборы в диалогах вели не к тому что предполагалось на основе этого самого диалога, и наконец почитав обсуждения и форумы я понял что таких допущений очень много, и они мешают целиком погрузится в игру.
review_english: Bring back the gods! Chicken Lives Matter! Freedom of movement for familiars! The game is certainly great, but for so many awards it is missing too much. PySy division Larian for this game is difficult for us, but we will say this is a feature for the sake of the plot and the freedom of the players! Choosing Silvanus is difficult for us, choosing a god for a non-cleric is optional in DnD5, so we won’t do it at all, because it’s difficult for us, choosing a worldview is difficult for us, but we’ll pass it off as a feature of freedom of choice, etc. etc. PyPySy I understand that PySy wrote as a fan of the Forgotten Realms, but even without this I have complaints, I bought it on pre-order, but didn’t play, waited for patches so as not to spoil the impression, in the end I put it off for 3 years, and recently I thought, well, now everything should be great! In 10 hours, I encountered a familiar seeping into a cave, and the impossibility of getting out of it, it turns out that we have this trick, familiars don’t go through locations, I promised the chicken to save her, but as it turned out, this was a predetermined deception, there is no such option, several times the choices in the dialogues did not lead to what was expected based on this very dialogue, and finally, after reading the discussions and forums, I realized that there are a lot of such assumptions, and they prevent you from completely immersing yourself in game.

review: Jugué este juego mas de 250 horas por otros medios, y ya era hora de comprarlo en Steam. Si no lo haz jugado...  que cojones esperas? Es EL juego de rol, personajes a los que de verdad les agarras cariño, historias muy profundas y escenarios que a mi la verdad me enamoraron desde mi 1era run. Y no solo eso, siempre veras algo nuevo cada que juegues desde 0. Actualizo, descubrí nuevamente zonas en el acto 2 y en el Underdark que no tenia idea de que existían. Descubrí 2 accesos nuevos al Underdark que no sabia de que estaban, además de loot en lugares que ni de cerca estuve por descubrir en runs anteriores. El combate cada vez es mas satisfactorio por la estrategia que se planifica antes de cada enfrentamiento. Es mi primera run 100% buena, y es increible como las acciones y decisiones tienen consecuencias a corto y largo plazo, 100% cine.

N’étant qu’homme mortel, ta céleste beauté
Me fit goûter la mort, la mort et la ruine
Pour de nouveau venir à l’immortalité.

Ton feu divin brûla mon essence mortelle,
Ton céleste m’éprit et me ravit aux Cieux,
Ton âme était divine et la mienne fut telle :
Déesse, tu me mis au rang des autres dieux.
Agrippa d'Aubigné

review:
[h1] AVIS GLOBAL[/h1] 
	[b]X[/b] Vivement recommandé
	☐ Globalement bon
	☐ Du bon et du mauvais
	☐ Pas recommandé

[b][u]AVIS DETAILLE[/u][/b]:
Ce jeu mérite totalement ces prix, c'est un chef d'oeuvre. Il a ses petits défauts mais il est absolument inégalé sur la qualité de son (ses) histoire, de ses personnages, la richesse de ses environnements, de son gameplay. La liberté d'action et de choix est incroyable et permet une vraie expérience de jeu de rôle. Le jeu se permet d'être long et pourtant je ne me lasse pas et j'en redemande à la fin. Heureusement, ce jeu est imaginé pour être rejoué et redécouvert avec des expériences variées.

[hr][/hr]
[h1] RAPPORT CONTENU/PRIX[/h1] 
	[b]X[/b] Généreux
	☐ Equilibré
	☐ Prix élevé

[b][u]TEMPS DE JEU[/u][/b]: > 100H

[hr][/hr]
[h1] GAME ARTS[/h1] 

- [b][u]SCENARIO[/u][/b]: Impressionnant ! La trame est assez "classique" en heroic fantasy, mais la richesse vient surtout des personnages qui nous accompagnent qui ont tous leur propre histoire et interactions avec l'histoire principale.

- [b][u]GRAPHISMES[/u][/b]: Très beaux, un travail monstrueux sur les détails de l'environnement.

- [b][u]BANDE SON[/u][/b]: Agréable mais le thème (exploité au maximum) devient répétitif à la longue.

- [b][u]CONCEPT[/u][/b]: La richesse d'un jeu de rôle sur table, mais en jeu vidéo. La promesse est très bien tenue !

[h1] GAME DESIGN[/h1] 

- [b][u]GAMEPLAY[/u][/b]: La personnalisation des personnages et des classes est très complète et permet de bons combos en combats et les interactions avec l'environnement sont réalistes. Un même problème peut être résolu de plusieurs façon différentes, y compris au travers des choix de dialogues.

- [b][u]DIFFICULTE[/u][/b]: Assez ardu en mode normal à moins de connaitre les détails des compétences et classes des persos. Heureusement le mode "facile/histoire" est plus accessible.

- [b][u]REJOUABILITE[/u][/b]: Très bonne pour explorer les embranchements de scénario et tester d'autres classes.

- [b][u]BUGS[/u][/b]: Encore quelques bugs restant, des glitchs et parfois des soft locks (il faut penser à faire régulièrement des sauvegardes rapides)

review:
BG3 isn't just a game; it feels like playing through a massive, high-budget cinematic movie where you're the director. I've taken courses where we studied cinematography, storyboards, animation etc., and [b] [u] Larian hit every criteria that I personally wanted, and expected from this game. [/u] [/b]

After nearly 120 hours, the absolute biggest highlight for me is the sheer level of immersion. The gorgeous cinematography, incredible graphics, and a phenomenal soundtrack completely pull you into the world. It makes you genuinely care about every single storyline, from the epic main quest down to the random side adventures.

If you love D&D, or if you're just looking for an unforgettable game with beautiful stories, stunning audio/visuals, and rewarding turn-based combat, buy this immediately. It is worth every single second.

review: Such an amazing and truly one of a kind game. It is D&D through and through; every choice, no matter how small it may seem at first, will have an unimaginable ripple effect at some point. It may be the game with the most value-for-money replayability possible; there is no single playthrough the same as the previous ones. So many classes and subclasses, so many decisions to make that will alter the course of the story in so many ways. The mechanics may seem strange at first if someone is not familiar with how tabletop RPGs are played, and the game could do a somewhat better job at explaining it. However it is fairly easy to understand and once someone gets the grasp of it it becomes so fun to make builds and try different things. A must play for everyone!

review:
Good, but overrated. I know people talk about this game like it's the second coming of CRPG's, but it's just a solidly good CRPG with some issues, particularly QoL issues and unresolved bugs.

The way the conversations and gameplay cover so many classes, options, skills, and playstyles is genuinely impressive, but that's the only really impressive thing about this game. The rest of it is stock CRPG roll-playing (as opposed to role playing) fare. Most of the game is wandering around looting things, disarming traps, picking locks, and getting in fights. It's more filler than meat.

Plus it's genuinely tiring to play sometimes. I often end up more tired after finishing a session than when I began. It's not a game to play to destress, especially when you don't have a lot of time to play. This is more of a weekend / holiday game, as it takes hours to play, for a surprisingly small amount of fun. Plus, the UI has issues and bugs, particularly if you use a controller primarily. The way stores  / trading work is bad too, as the filtering options aren't great, and there's no way to mark or filter out "junk" items the way there is with almost every other game like this. A lot of items that should stack don't stack either.

Lastly, the game's systems themselves are more fun than the actual content of the game. I haven't gotten to Act 3 yet, but Act 1 is just okay, and Act 2 is a total slog, and really overstays its welcome and is very one-note and kind of boring. If this were compared to a tabletop game, it would be one where the DM railroads you into a "everything is shadowy and undead for 100 hours whether you like it or not". I actually quit my first playthrough due to Act 2, and only came back due to youtube joke videos about the rest of the game, which looked interesting.

I would say get this game on sale, and try it for free if you can from a friend's Steam Share or whatnot. If you like CRPG's you'll probably like this game, but if you don't like CRPG's, I honestly don't think BG3 will change your mind. It's good at what it is (to an extent) but I wouldn't say it's good enough to convert people that normally don't play games like this.

I know I'm recommending this game, but only with caveats. If there was a thumbs sideways option, I'd pick that.

review:
I've got some odd near 500 hours on this game - though I feel like I've def played longer. Either way, I love how story dense this is and how you can play this for 500h + and still find new things about it, new dialogue, and new hidden little things. 

I'm on my third replay and it's the first since the patch where they added new subclasses. 

Not only that, but it runs so well on my Mac. I can run it with Ultra graphics, no issues. My only complaint so to speak is the dial when playing with a controller, but even so, it doesn't detract too much. I love this game, and years later I still talk about it.

review: La vida de un rollero es sencilla en muchos aspectos, arriesgamos poco, y tenemos poder sobre aquellos que ofrecen su trabajo y servicio a nuestro juicio. Prosperamos con las críticas negativas – divertidas de escribir y leer- pero la triste verdad que debemos afrontar, es que en el gran orden de las cosas, cualquier basura tiene más significado que lo que deja ver nuestra crítica. Pero en ocasiones el verdadero rollero si se arriesga cada vez que descubre y defiende algo nuevo… el mundo suele ser cruel con el nuevo talento, las nuevas creaciones… lo nuevo, necesita amigos. Anoche experimenté algo nuevo, una extraordinaria partida de una fuente singular e inesperada, decir sólo que el Baldur's Gate 3 y su creador han desafiado mis prejuicios sobre la el buen rolleo subestimaría la realidad. Me han tocado en lo más profundo. En el pasado, jamás oculté mi desdén por la famosa descripción de Larian Studios "Crear videojuegos de clase mundial" Pero al fin me doy cuenta de lo que quiso decir en realidad: "No cualquier videojuego puede ser de clase mundial, pero un videojuego de clase mundial puede venir de cualquier lado". Es difícil imaginar un origen más humilde que el del genio que ahora dirige Larian Studios, y quien, en opinión de este rollero, es nada menos que el mejor estudio de Bélgica. Pronto volveré a un juego de Larian… ansioso.

Me han tocado en lo más profundo. En el pasado, jamás oculté mi desdén por la famosa descripción de Larian Studios "Crear videojuegos de clase mundial" Pero al fin me doy cuenta de lo que quiso decir en realidad "No cualquier videojuego puede ser de clase mundial, pero un videojuego de clase mundial puede venir de cualquier lado". Es difícil imaginar un origen más humilde que el del genio que ahora dirige Larian Studios, y quien, en opinión de este rollero, es nada menos que el mejor estudio de Bélgica. Pronto volveré a un juego de Larian ansioso.
review_english: The life of a roller is simple in many aspects, we risk little, and we have power over those who offer their work and service at our discretion. We thrive on negative reviews that are fun to write and read - but the sad truth we must face is that in the grand scheme of things, any garbage has more meaning than our review lets on. But sometimes the true roller takes a risk every time he discovers and defends something new, the world is usually cruel to new talent, new creations, new things, he needs friends. Last night I experienced something new, an extraordinary game from a singular and unexpected source, to say only that Baldur's Gate 3 and its creator have challenged my prejudices about good vibes would understate the reality. They have touched me deeply. In the past, I have never hidden my disdain for Larian Studios' famous description "Creating world-class video games." But I finally realize what he really meant: "Not just any video game can be world-class, but a world-class video game can come from anywhere." It is difficult to imagine a more humble origin than that of the genius who now runs Larian Studios, and who, in this roller's opinion, is nothing less than the best studio in Belgium. I'll be back to an eager game of Larian soon.

review: Baldur's Gate 3 has become my favourite game ever. Not only do I love the D&D mechanics but the story, combat, world and characters is amazing. I've played through multiple times and still consistently discover new dialogue, new ways to play and new easter eggs. The way devs have allowed the modding community to operate is also special and adds endless options for playability to a game that was already well done in that respect. The companion system is interesting and adds another level of joy to the game though could use a few tweaks (which some mods take care of). The choices for voice actors were spot on and they have done such a great job in how they've portrayed the characters. I recommend this game to any D&D or RPG fan especially anyone into fantasy and character development. If you loved the choose your adventure books growing up, this is for you.

review:
This is my favourite game of all time. I had never played DnD before but was always curious about it, my girlfriend, however, is very into it. We bought this game on release to try "DnD as a video game" and let me tell you, it blew all of my expectations out of the water.

The characters and their individual stories are so enthralling that you'll want to do multiple playthroughs just to see how they react to different choices. The combat is amazing and really gets you thinking strategically, pushing you to try out many different builds and playstyles. The world is beautifully and painstakingly handcrafted and rewards you extremly well for going off the beaten path to explore. There is so much to do in this game i seriosuly doubt you could do it all in your first playthrough without looking up to see where everything is. There are many long term meaninful choices, something you did in act one you will see the results of many hours later in act three

It stays very faithful to the tabletop while also making some welcome QOL additions. such as making potions a bonus action, or slightly reworking some of the less than optimal classes to breathe new life into them and making them genuinely fun to play. The sheer number of choices you have and the ways you can shape how the story unfolds is just incredible. i have replayed this game countless times and can forsee many more to come.

My only issue is that the devs have said they won't be making any DLC which is honestly heartbreaking

review:
There truly is no other game like it. A playthrough can easily last over 100 hours, with every moment of that playthrough feeling fresh and engaging. 

There are so many places to explore that even on your 4th playthrough you’ll find a new area you haven’t seen before, possibly even holding hours of content you’ve never experienced yet. Every part of this game tells a story, even the smallest of things you find will have their own small part of the overarching story. Like a random npc you find in the first few hours later becomes part of a large quest 100 hours later, or a book found in a random basement the that only makes sense later on. 

In terms of gameplay it really is up to your imagination. You can go in guns blazing into every fight, or you can assassinate enemies 1 by 1, gather them all into one room and blow them up, pick enemies up and toss them into danger. Most fights can even be avoided by just talking your way out of it, you might even end up making friends with them. There are a huge range of spells and weapons and armour, allowing for diverse builds across multiple classes. The game even has a built in mod browser, allowing for even more replayability.

You can’t say you’ve experienced the peak of gaming until you’ve played this, it genuinely is THAT good

review:
I haven't played this exact type of game before, but I really enjoyed this.  Character development, writing and especially the voice acting was great and often made choosing even seemingly mundane dialogue options a test of morality.  I probably spent as long staring at the screen deciding what to do as doing it, in a good way.  It was also neat to see how early decisions played out meaingfully as the game progressed as well.

Turn-based combat with full freedom of movement was a highlight for me.  This made combat tactical using environment and all of the capabilities of each character while I enjoyed my tea.  Class choices while controlling a full party allows for a cohesive team dynamic with each character playing a role.  While some classes and abilities seemed to dominate at certain points in the game, by and large, the balance was good as I leaned into what each class offered the party as their levels progressed.  You really do want a healer in your party though (most likely a tanky cleric), so helpful when scraping through tight encounters.

As a small gripe, while I was generally immersed throughout, in the final act, a couple of unexpected outcomes and tricky quests had me googling.  That said, I recommend if you're feeling like you've blundered through some areas as I did, just keep going for immersions sake.  You'll get to the ending regardless, just along a different path than you intended.

Overall, I put BG3 on top of any other game I've played quite frankly, with enjoyable moments coming far more frequently than any negatives.

review:
Baldur's Gate 3 ruined RPGs for me.

After spending hundreds of hours in Faerûn, it's hard to go back to games where every quest has one solution, every choice leads to the same outcome, and your companions feel like quest dispensers instead of actual people.

BG3 gives you an absurd amount of freedom. Want to talk your way through a problem? Go ahead. Sneak around it? Sure. Set a trap, throw a goblin off a cliff, polymorph someone into a sheep, or accidentally start a fight that changes the entire course of your playthrough? The game usually just shrugs and says, "Yeah, that works."

The companions are some of the best I've ever seen in an RPG. They have their own goals, flaws, personalities, and opinions, and they feel like genuine traveling companions rather than NPCs waiting for their turn to speak. By the end of the journey, I cared about their stories almost as much as my own.

The world is packed with secrets, meaningful choices, and consequences that can show up hours later when you've completely forgotten about them. Few games reward curiosity as much as Baldur's Gate 3 does.

Of course, it's not perfect. Turn-based combat isn't for everyone, and a single playthrough barely scratches the surface of what the game has to offer. But that's also part of its greatest strength—every playthrough feels like your own story.

**Pros:**

* Incredible player freedom
* Outstanding companions and writing
* Meaningful choices and consequences
* Deep combat system
* Massive replay value

**Cons:**

* Turn-based combat may not appeal to everyone
* You'll immediately start planning a second playthrough
* Sleep becomes optional

**Overall: 10/10**

Most RPGs let you play a character.

Baldur's Gate 3 lets you create a story.

And somehow, it remembers nearly every decision you make along the way.

review:
Se você procura um RPG de verdade, Baldur's Gate 3 é um prato cheio e entrega muito mais do que se imagina. O jogo oferece uma liberdade impressionante, permitindo que cada decisão influencie sua jornada e crie uma história única.

Outro ponto forte é o enorme fator replay. Existem diversas possibilidades, escolhas, consequências e finais diferentes, o que incentiva novas campanhas para experimentar caminhos completamente distintos, seja jogando sozinho ou com amigos, mesmo sem ter zerado o jogo ainda estou atualmente no Ato 2 posso dizer que estou adorando a experiência. É um daqueles jogos que dá vontade de jogar várias vezes para descobrir novas histórias, formar grupos diferentes e ver como suas escolhas mudam o mundo ao seu redor. 🌷

review_english: If you're looking for a real RPG, Baldur's Gate 3 is a complete dish and delivers much more than you might imagine. The game offers impressive freedom, allowing each decision to influence your journey and create a unique story. Another strong point is the huge replay factor. There are several possibilities, choices, consequences and different endings, which encourages new campaigns to experience completely different paths, whether playing alone or with friends, even without having finished the game, I'm still in Act 2, I can say that I'm loving the experience. It's one of those games that makes you want to play over and over again to discover new stories, form different groups and see how your choices change the world around you.

review:
Le tour par tour me freinait au départ, mais l’univers me donnait vraiment envie d’essayer. J’ai finalement sauté le pas et je ne regrette absolument pas mon expérience.

La plus grande force du jeu, c’est sa liberté. Rarement un RPG m’a donné autant de possibilités dans les quêtes, les dialogues ou la manière d’aborder les situations. Chaque décision semble avoir un impact, et le jeu récompense constamment la créativité du joueur.

En revanche, Baldur's Gate 3 n’est pas un jeu qui prend le joueur par la main. Il est assez complexe à appréhender au début, demande du temps pour comprendre ses mécaniques et nécessite de beaucoup lire. Certains combats peuvent également être exigeants si l’on ne maîtrise pas bien les systèmes du jeu.

C’est un véritable jeu de rôle à l’ancienne, riche, dense et profond. Si vous êtes prêt à vous investir, l’expérience est exceptionnelle.

Seuls quelques défauts l’empêchent d’atteindre la perfection : une courbe d’apprentissage parfois abrupte, quelques problèmes de rythme selon les actes et une interface qui peut sembler chargée pour les nouveaux joueurs. Mais ce sont des défauts mineurs face aux qualités du jeu.

Certains combats peuvent également être exigeants si l on ne maîtrise pas bien les systèmes du jeu. C est un véritable jeu de rôle à l ancienne, riche, dense et profond. Si vous êtes prêt à vous investir, l expérience est exceptionnelle. Seuls quelques défauts l empêchent d atteindre la perfection une courbe d apprentissage parfois abrupte, quelques problèmes de rythme selon les actes et une interface qui peut sembler chargée pour les nouveaux joueurs. Mais ce sont des défauts mineurs face aux qualités du jeu.
review_english: The turn-based approach slowed me down at first, but the universe really made me want to try. I finally took the plunge and I absolutely do not regret my experience. The greatest strength of the game is its freedom. Rarely has an RPG given me so many possibilities in quests, dialogues or the way of approaching situations. Every decision seems to have an impact, and the game constantly rewards the player's creativity. On the other hand, Baldur's Gate 3 is not a game that takes the player by the hand. It is quite complex to understand at the beginning, takes time to understand its mechanics and requires a lot of reading. Some combat can also be demanding if you don't master the game's systems well. It's a real old-fashioned role-playing game, rich, dense and deep. If you are ready to invest, the experience is exceptional. Only a few flaws prevent it from achieving perfection: a sometimes steep learning curve, some rhythm problems depending on the actions and an interface that can seem busy for new players. But these are minor flaws compared to the qualities of the game.

review: I’ve already gone through three runs and it still keeps me hooked with its rich story, memorable companions, and tactical combat that always feels rewarding. The world is packed with detail and secrets that make exploring a joy, and every choice feels like it really matters. At the same time, I still see Divinity: Original Sin 2 as its own beast, with more room for free will and player agency, which to me makes DOS2 the stronger game overall. For now, I’m happily bouncing between BG3 and DOS2 while waiting for the next Divinity game to arrive.

review: This game rules. I grew up playing KOTOR and KOTOR 2, eventually mastering them both. I heard about this game and was intrigued because of the fantasy elements even though I didn't know a single thing about D&D so I purchased it on a whim because of all the hype it was getting. At first I was uneasy at the idea of turn based combat but as soon as I realized how similar it was to KOTOR I got immediately hooked. Finding out that KOTOR is based on 3rd edition D&D d20 rules blew my mind so it was an easy transition to figuring this game out. I literally can't stop playing this game, it's my #1 most played game probably ever.

review: I highly suggest to play this game. I literally fell in love with it. The story, the plot are masterfully written, in both good and evil ways and for everything in the middle. NPC's background and characterization are so good you cannot help but fall in love with at least one of them. Class customizations and mods are funny; the multiple choices you can make and change every run it's absurd and the quantity of lore you can find while exploring all of the maps it allows you to immerse even more in this beautiful fantasy world! Last but not least, even if we are talking about a fantasy story, behind every choice, behind every character, there is humanity: not like the the simple "hero" or "antagonist" stereotypes, in all of them, you can see the same spark as we human being have: fear, anger, hope. For me, it's a 10/10.

review: After my first two runs, I was scared to put this as my favorite game ever because I thought it may be just recency bias. After two years and four more runs, I think it's safe to say it deserves my number one spot. This game is the perfect blend of serious storytelling but also being extremely fun. All the systems in this game are well thought out and work very well. The DnD RNG for everything adds a nice level of unpredictability. The replayability of this game is one of one. Each run I did was actually unique (whether in a big or small way) and I even learned new stuff or ways to do things in each one. The only negatives I have is there are a bunch of bugs and jankyiness that are a little frustrating. There are a bunch of classes but some are clearly much better than others. I'm sure I could scan a guide and make one of the bad classes good through cheese. I just feel like it shouldn't come to that, I should be able to pick and play how I want without feeling way weaker than the premier classes. Those complaints are just drops in an endless sea of enjoyment though. For each moment you may run into buggy frustration, you'll run into 50 peaks moments that make you forgive everything.

review:
This is the first review I’ve ever written on Steam, and Baldur’s Gate 3 absolutely deserves it.

I still remember playing the original Baldur’s Gate more than 20 years ago, and even then, I was impressed by the quality of the game.

Baldur’s Gate 3 is the best game I have played in my entire life — and believe me, I’ve spent countless hours playing all kinds of games. I also experienced it in co-op, and the other player had the exact same opinion.

I honestly have no criticism. This game is simply perfect.

Incredible work by Larian.

review:
For me this hits exactly like The Last of Us, where it is very clear that this game was made with care and love to the point of being an absolute work of art. The story is captivating and instantly draws me in, and I can't wait to see what happens to the characters. 

but I never will, because actually PLAYING the game is a frustrating, un-fun slog for me, and I feel like I'm so close to grasping the things the game wants me to learn and yet it's still just out of reach. I feel like I'm constantly making dumb mistakes trying to understand the various systems to get my characters builds online only to be fed into fights that are unwinnable or should have been avoided or handled in some creative way that I'm too much of a clownass to understand 

in short, it feels too much like life

This is my first CRPG game. Once I get familiar with it, the rest is fun. But there is one thing I need to remind you. This game really requires intelligence and carefulness to play. The amount of information in the text is ridiculous, but the story is worthy of you who may need nearly half an hour to think about a conversation or the content of a book. In addition to the extremely high playability of the profession, the player's degree of freedom is also extremely high. Each of your ideas can produce different results and affect the direction of the main line. The correlation between the NPC branch lines and the main line is also very interesting. It is very immersive after playing. Highly recommended.

review:
I was doing some initial checking on reviews and was surprised when I haven't reviewed this.

BG3 is frankly a once in a generational triumph of game design. Taking something as open and freeing as TTRPGs and somehow compacting and molding a videogame into that space is so incredibly, deeply impressive. This game is just good. Really tremendously good. That is my short sell.

From the writing to the characters to the music and voice acting. Everything that makes a great narrative that feels weighty and alive is on full display here. People I know who have no interest in videogames, almost at all, flock to this game. I do a playthrough every few months work shopping insane dual-classes and bullshitting with my friends. It's a truly wonderful adaption of a TTRPGs ruleset and I wish more great systems out there would get the love and care this game has in it. 

Larian is truly in a league of their own with their ambition and efforts and I eagerly await their following titles. Gaming isn't dead friends you just need to play games like this.

review: An absolute Masterpiece in my opinion- The characters are wonderfully written, and I still find myself listening to all the dialogue, even after 800 hours. Quests are varied, interesting, and I've always found or discovered something new in each adventure, be it commentary or a quest or a new loot spot. I come back for more every few months; BG3 has earned a place in my heart, gotten me through some tough times, and will forever have a place on my hard drives. Even for those who have never played d&d, its a fantastic game and a shining example of what an RPG can and should be- Interesting, exciting, but also a great way to relax and very open with choices that matter. Top 3 games in my library

Divinity 2 is the better game, BG3 is the better DnD (5e) sim.

review: Besides being daunting for a brand new solo player having to learn their class AND 3 Companions, this game is REALLY amazing! There is an INSANE amount of discovery/exploration that will keep you busy for hours alongside the Main and Side Quests. This was my first turn-based combat game and I'm happy to say I enjoy the freedom to play out a fight as fast or slow as is needed for me at the time. The roleplay? VERY fun and dynamic. Love the graphics! For the first time I've actually started a 2nd playthrough of a game in order to get done what I missed and do some interactions differently. DEFINITELY WORTH THE BUY!

review:
Definitiv kann ich es empfehlen. Für Rollenspiel und Dungeons & Dragons Fans ein Meister- und Kunstwerk. Was Larian Studios hier erschaffen haben ist unbeschreiblich gut. 

Grafik: 10/10
Gameplay 10/10
Story 10/10

Alles bis auf jede kleine Nebenmission fühlt sich als Ganzes an. Die Freiheiten welche dieses Spiel im Gameplay bietet sind massig und der Wiederspielwert dieses Spiels ist ebenfalls da, denn in einem Durchgang alles zu sehen und zu machen ist eigentlich unmöglich. Ich liebe es. 
Der Hammer ist wenn man möchte kann man das auch noch im Multiplayer erleben und Modding wird hier sogar unterstützt. Es ist der Hammer. Auch nach drei Jahren noch unvergleichlich spitze.

Ich habe gesprochen!

Joguem esse jogo com calma. Degustem cada momento, porque realmente ninguém está exagerando ao dizer que é uma obra-prima.

review: Solid D&D style adventure with well crafted storytelling and well written character development.  The one downside here is that it lacks a more expanded world to export characters through.  Decades ago some old D&D computer games let you import characters from the earlier games (a really remarkable feature for the time) to carry them forward instead of forcing you to start fresh.  I'd love that to be possible in future games, and honestly it needs some expansion releases.  But the game itself is great and I'm glad I bought it even as a stand alone.

review:
There's a lot to love about this game. The story is good, the characters are great, there's care and attention put into how the game was created, and so on.
However, there’s a lot that frustrates me about the game too. Positioning in combat is often annoying. So many fights are designed with all the enemies already placed strategically on higher ground, and it feels like my melee characters always have to waste a round (or two or three) getting into position to actually contribute (to the point that I tend to just go with archers and casters). I also dislike how D&D is designed around the idea that heroes should fail at actions so much. When you combine that with ability checks and rolls for just about everything you can imagine, it makes the results of just about anything to be swingy.
