review:
Do you play DND? Did you ever write stories for yourself in school, or post in boards like Archive of Our Own? Do you enjoy smut? Get Baldurs Gate 3 and thank me later. The game is great, brilliant graphics, and amazing story. Your choices matter, but just because they matter doesn't mean you cannot reshuffle the deck to get the results you want.

At this point, three years after release, if you haven't heard anything about this game it may be because you have turned away from tactical turnbased games. Give this game a chance, the story and characters are absolutely amazing, and that first time you absolutely body someone in a turn by doing something outside the box, you will feel like an actual hero. Seriously, there is a reason this is in my top 5 games of all time

review:
神作 货真价实的年度游戏 太绝了  99/100
pros.
优点说不完，就是个第九艺术，各方面惊艳，有着玩游戏想得到的所有，惊人的细节丰富度，叹为观止的文本量，无比自由的选择与rp，抓耳的音乐，角色有趣的灵魂，有深度的故事。让我真真切切的感受到制作组无比用心的塑造这么一个有魅力的世界。
cons.
CRPG给了这个世界无与伦比的自由度与丰富度，但同时确实会让战斗变得拖沓一点，有多少人愿意在这个浮躁的世界静下来品这个游戏呢。我其实一直都不喜欢回合制，因为我接触游戏比较晚，难熟悉非连贯的战斗，但BG3我觉得是不一样的，因为每次战斗有着不同的地形，不同特性的敌人，所以要用不同的策略去应对，策略让这个游戏的战斗系统变得特别值得推敲。还有就是海量内容带来的另一个问题，bug，我三年后游玩，还是一周目遇到了两次闪退，一些小bug，但是问题不大。有个观点我觉得很对，如果没bug，可能反而说明这游戏深度不够吧，哈哈哈哈。

review_english: A masterpiece, a real game of the year. It’s amazing. 99 100 pros. There are endless advantages. It’s the ninth art. It’s amazing in every aspect. It has everything you want in a game. Amazing richness of details. Amazing amount of text. Incomparable freedom of choice and RP. Catching music. Interesting souls of the characters. Deep story. I really feel that the production team has put great care into creating such a charming world. cons. CRPG gives the world unparalleled freedom and richness, but at the same time it does make the battle a little bit protracted. How many people are willing to calm down and enjoy this game in this impetuous world? In fact, I have never liked the turn-based system because I came into the game relatively late and it is difficult to get familiar with non-consecutive battles. But I think BG3 is different because each battle has different terrain and enemies with different characteristics, so different strategies must be used to deal with it. The strategy makes the combat system of this game particularly worthy of scrutiny. There is also another problem caused by the massive content, bug. I played it three years later I still encountered two crashes in one game. There are some small bugs but they are not major problems. I have a point of view that I think is right. If there are no bugs, it may mean that the game is not deep enough, hahahaha.

review:
There's a section of the game where you're supposed to save a couple of prisoners (gondians) and the path-finding is horrible! This section ruins the entire game for me. I've tried over and over and over, I've watched tutorials and turned to forums for help but what can you do when the NPC's simply don't work? They run around like headless chickens! This part gave me a headache a week ago which made me uninstall. I spent a whole night re-downloading it and then the entire morning trying to get this ♥♥♥♥ show of a quest to function but nope, it does. not. work.. I'm so invested now in this particular mission and don't want to finish the game unless I can find a way to make it work.
Edit:
Okay I finally did it, it took 50-100 attempts and several days but the gondians are finally saved! I'm relieved and emotionally drained. This part of the game is best avoided but hey, it is possible to complete.

review:
If there is an RPG that every gamer should play it's this, simple enough for anyone to learn, while also pushing the player with combat encounters that require some thought (specially in tactician mode). If you aren't partial to difficult combat, there is a difficulty setting tailor made for those who do not wish to indulge in it while still enabling role-playing. 

Overall the story is great, even if it can fall off near the end due to budget/time constraints that the developers faced.

The characters are all memorable and lovable (milage may vary between characters), each coming with their own rich backstory, dynamic personalities, and gripping characters arcs, the party roster in this game is 10/10, and I frankly could not imagine a different set of main characters for this game. 

Full mod support just seals the deal for me, while it may not be as extensive as something like Fallout New Vegas or Skyrim, it is still a great way to lengthen the life span of the game, on top of the support that the developers gave the game. If there is one negative about the mod support is that because of the way Baldur's Gate 3 was made, mod content is a bit limited due to how difficult it can be to create new level areas, at least to my knowledge.

If there was ever a standard for RPGs to follow, it's Baldur's Gate 3

review:
The Verdict: A Masterclass in Player Agency and Narrative Depth
After nearly 90 hours embedded in the Forgotten Realms, it’s clear that Baldur’s Gate 3 isn’t just a faithful adaptation of Dungeons & Dragons 5e—it is a high-water mark for the entire RPG genre. Larian Studios has built a digital sandbox that somehow manages to feel deeply personal, incredibly chaotic, and meticulously structured all at once.

If you are looking for a game that respects your intelligence, rewards your curiosity, and consequences your lapses in judgment, this is it.

Key Pillars of the Experience
Unparalleled Tactical Freedom: The combat engine is a brilliant translation of tabletop mechanics. Position, verticality, and environmental manipulation aren't just gimmicks; they are survival tools. Whether you’re setting up a perfect ambush from the rafters, triggering a massive chain reaction with grease and fire, or simply shoving an elite boss off a cliff to their demise, the game constantly says "yes" to your tactical ingenuity.

A Living, Reactive Narrative: The writing and voice acting are phenomenal, but the true triumph is the reactivity. Your choices—from major faction alliances down to minor dialogue shrugs—ripple across acts. The companions feel less like scripted digital followers and more like a volatile, deeply human crew with their own agendas, moral thresholds, and trauma to unpack.

Meticulous Map Design: Every square inch of the world feels deliberate. There is zero filler content. You’ll set out to resolve a main questline only to find yourself deep in an underground cavern three hours later, unraveling a completely separate, fully voiced narrative arc because you decided to look behind a hidden wall.

The 89-Hour Perspective
At this milestone, the scale of the game is still staggering. Even when you think you’ve mastered the systems, the narrative shifts or a new environment forces you to rethink your entire approach. It handles high-stakes political intrigue, cosmic horror, and intimate character moments with equal grace.

It runs beautifully on high-end hardware, rendering the dark, gritty corners of the Underdark and the sprawling urban vistas with stunning detail. The audio design—especially the soaring orchestral score and crisp positioning—keeps you completely locked into the atmosphere.

Final Thoughts
Baldur’s Gate 3 is a rare achievement. It demands your time, but it pays you back in pure quality, emergent storytelling, and unforgettable tactical victories. Whether you're a veteran DM or completely new to turn-based strategy, this is an absolute mandatory play.

10/10 — The gold standard.

review:
Voy a ser directo: Es el mejor juego que he jugado en los últimos 10 años fácilmente, y he jugado unos cuantos. 

La historia, la música, las mecánicas, los personajes (LOS PERSONAJES!), todo encaja como un puzzle. El juego te hace sentir parte del mundo y cada pequeña acción suma, hasta algo tan insignificante como evitar que a una niña le muerda una serpiente acaba desencadenado algo más tarde o más temprano.

Si bien mecánicamente puede abrumar, como dice el refrán, la práctica hace al maestro, y eso refuerza la sensación de crecimiento, tal como ocurriría en una partida de rol de mesa en la que poco a poco vas mejorando la ficha de personaje y en la que si tienes un buen master, imaginación y una pizca de suerte, todo es posible.

Sumérgete en Faerun, no te arrepentirás.

Sumérgete en Faerun, no te arrepentirás.
review_english: I'm going to be direct. It's easily the best game I've played in the last 10 years, and I've played a few. The story, the music, the mechanics, the characters (THE CHARACTERS!), everything fits together like a puzzle. The game makes you feel part of the world and every small action adds up, even something as insignificant as preventing a girl from being bitten by a snake ends up triggering something sooner or later. Although mechanically it can be overwhelming, as the saying goes, practice makes perfect, and that reinforces the feeling of growth, just as it would happen in a tabletop role-playing game in which little by little you improve your character sheet and in which if you have a good master, imagination and a pinch of luck, anything is possible. Immerse yourself in Faerun, you won't regret it.

review: Muhtemelen Steam'de yazdığım ilk inceleme. Yani pc gaming hariç Ps1'den beri oyun oynayan biri olarak şunu söyleyebilirim ki, bu oyun oynadığım hiçbir oyuna benzemiyor. İlk başlarda zorlanabilir yeni başlayan arkadaşlar ama Act 1'ı bitirdikten sonra muhakkak devam edin. Asıl oyun orada başlıyor. Sinematikler, hikaye anlatımı, müzikler diyecek bir söz yok. Best of the best yani. Şu an second run dönüyorum ve oyunun mükemmelliğiyse şurada başlıyor. Oyunu her oynadığınızda bambaşka side storyler keşfedebiliyorsunuz. Hiç görmediğiniz npcleri görebilip bambaşka farklı diyaloglarla hikayeyi bambaşka yerlere evirebiliyorsunuz. Bunun yanı sıra hiç dahil olmadığınız hikayeli karakterleri de partinize alarak onların bambaşka hikayelerini görme şansınız oluşuyor. Bu oyun ilmek ilmek işlenmiş bir oyun. Neredeyse her karakterin ve her npcnin birbiriyle iç içe geçmiş bağları var. Aslında bu oyuna oyun demek ayıp olur gibi, film gibi, fantastik roman gibi bir şey bu. Oynayın, oynattırın.

review_english: Probably the first review I've written on Steam. So, as someone who has been playing games since PS1, excluding PC gaming, I can say that this game is unlike any game I have played. It may be difficult for beginners at first, but after completing Act 1, definitely continue. The real game starts there. There is nothing to say about cinematics, storytelling, music. Best of the best, that is. I'm currently on my second run and the perfection of the game starts here. You can discover completely different side stories every time you play the game. You can see NPCs you have never seen before and evolve the story into completely different places with completely different dialogues. In addition, you have the chance to see completely different stories of characters you have never been involved with by bringing them to your party. This game is a game that has been processed step by step. Almost every character and every NPC has intertwined connections with each other. In fact, it would be a shame to call this game a game; it is like a movie or a fantasy novel. Play it, make it play.

review:
Шедевр, который возвращает веру в игровую индустрию

Знаете это редкое чувство из детства, когда ты полностью пропадаешь для реального мира, потому что игра поглотила тебя целиком? С BG3 я снова его испытал.

Larian создали не просто RPG, а настоящий симулятор свободы. Здесь можно заболтать до смерти босса, превратиться в кота, чтобы пролезть в тайник, или случайно запустить гнома из катапульты в стратосферу. Сюжет держит в напряжении, а вариативность поражает: твои решения реально аукаются спустя десятки часов.

Ну и, конечно, персонажи. Они настолько живые, сложные и харизматичные, что к концу приключения они становятся тебе как родные (а за Астариона или Шэдоухарт вообще хочется пойти войной на весь мир).

10 оторванных рук Гейла из 10. Если вы сомневаетесь, брать или нет — берите. Это классика на века.

review_english: A masterpiece that restores faith in the gaming industry Do you know that rare feeling from childhood when you completely disappear from the real world because the game has absorbed you entirely? With BG3 I experienced it again. Larian created not just an RPG, but a real freedom simulator. Here you can chat the boss to death, turn into a cat to crawl into a hiding place, or accidentally launch a gnome from a catapult into the stratosphere. The plot keeps you in suspense, and the variability is amazing; your decisions really come back to haunt you after tens of hours. And, of course, the characters. They are so lively, complex and charismatic that by the end of the adventure they become like family to you (and you actually want to go to war with the whole world for Astarion or Shadowheart). 10 severed hands of Gale out of 10. If you are in doubt whether to take it or not, take it. This is a classic for the ages.

review: Eine epische Geschichte in wunderschönen handgebauten Landschaften die zum endlosen Erkunden einladen. Hinzu kommt eine musikalischen Untermalung die perfekt mit den Kämpfen und dem Spielgeschehen abgestimmt ist. Aber nicht nur die Hauptstory ist spannend sondern auch die Nebenquests und die Geschichten der Begleiter können überzeugen. Das alles schafft eine fesselnde Atmosphäre die ich schon lange nicht mehr erlebt habe. Eigentlich wäre ich jetzt mit meiner Rezension durch, wenn da nicht das D&D Regelwerk wäre. Und genau das ist mein großes Problem mit Baldurs Gate 3. Was bei den Dialogen mit den Würfeln noch einen bestimmten Charme hat (so kann sich jedes Durchspielen komplett unterscheiden und wie im echten Leben läuft es eben nicht immer so wie man es gerne hätte) ist dies bei den Kämpfen eine echte Spaßbremse und ein Ärgernis zugleich. Weil Glück ein größere Rolle als der vorhandene Skill spielt. Weil die besten Waffen und die beste Vorbereitung nichts bringen, wenn man nicht zum Zug kommt oder Fehlschläge selbst bei einer Trefferwahrscheinlichkeit von über 90% keine Seltenheit sind. Dies führt oft zu einer Todesspirale bei den Helden, der man machtlos gegenüber steht. So ist es zwar schön wenn man im Kampf mit Objekten in der Umgebung interagieren oder Zaubereffekte kombinieren kann, aber gleichzeitig hat man die Wirkung einzelner Zauber im Vergleich zu Divinity gestutzt. Vor allem gegen Ende des zweiten Kapitels verschärft sich die ganze Problematik, weil der Schwierigkeitsgrad deutlich steigt. Einzige Möglichkeit den Frust zu minimieren ist häufiges Speichern in den Kämpfen. Dadurch geht aber in meinen Augen die Dynamik verloren und es fühlt sich teilweise nicht richtig an. Ein weiterer Kritikpunkt ist die schwache Performance im letzten Drittel. In Baldurs Gate fallen die FPS an manchen Stellen auf auf weit unter 60 und das auf einem System mit einer RTX 4080. Ob Larian Studios hier nach über zwei Jahren noch nachbessert, kann ich mir nicht vorstellen. Mein endgültiges Fazit: Trotz der genannten Kritikpunkte ist Baldurs Gate 3 ein Meisterwerk, das für lange Zeit der Maßstab für alle Rollenspiele sein wird. Würde ich nach 150 Stunden eine weiteren Durchgang wagen? Eher nicht! Kann man das Spiel empfehlen? Grundsätzlich ja, aber nur wenn man frustresistent ist und viel Freizeit für das Spiel opfern kann.

review_english: An epic story set in beautiful hand-crafted landscapes that invite endless exploration. There is also a musical background that is perfectly coordinated with the fights and the gameplay. But not only the main story is exciting, the side quests and the stories of the companions are also convincing. All of this creates a captivating atmosphere that I haven't experienced in a long time. Actually, I would be done with my review now if it weren't for the D D rules. And that's exactly my big problem with Baldur's Gate 3. What still has a certain charm in the dialogues with the dice (every playthrough can be completely different and, like in real life, things don't always go the way you would like), this is a real hindrance to the fun and an annoyance at the same time in the fights. Because luck plays a bigger role than existing skills. Because the best weapons and the best preparation are of no use if you don't get a chance or failures are not uncommon, even with a hit probability of over 90. This often leads to a death spiral for the heroes, which they are powerless to face. So it's nice to be able to interact with objects in the environment or combine spell effects during combat, but at the same time the effect of individual spells has been reduced compared to Divinity. The whole problem gets worse, especially towards the end of the second chapter, because the level of difficulty increases significantly. The only way to minimize frustration is to save frequently during battles. In my opinion, the dynamic is lost and it sometimes doesn't feel right. Another point of criticism is the weak performance in the last third. In Baldur's Gate the FPS drops to well below 60 in some places and that's on a system with an RTX 4080. I can't imagine whether Larian Studios will improve this after more than two years. My final conclusion Despite the criticisms mentioned, Baldur's Gate 3 is a masterpiece that will be the benchmark for all role-playing games for a long time. Would I try another playthrough after 150 hours? Rather not! Can you recommend the game? Basically yes, but only if you are resistant to frustration and can sacrifice a lot of free time for the game.

review:
你必须放下手中的一切事情来体验无敌的博德之门3
某种意义上是拯救本人游戏荒的完美作品，ps4ps5时代都体验了之后，对很多3a属于一开场角色上一句话说完我就知道后面要发生什么了，所以近几年反而玩日本人做的文字游戏和像素游戏比较多（题外话细胞神曲其实还挺好玩的，不看实况光玩游戏一些演出也很好）
但是偶尔也会想玩一些有意思的画面精美一点的作品，最符合口味的巫师3也太老了而且已经玩过n次，就在博主陷入迷茫的时候博3发售了，画面和演出无可挑剔，剧情虽然没有那么有意思但是表现正常，且完全没有一些令人不适的内容，并且在各个细节上都打磨得非常完善，唯一可以挑出毛病的就是和神界原罪2一样有点虎头蛇尾
希望拉瑞安下一部作品也能继续体现对游戏的热爱
在ps5上购入了数字版，前不久在pc也补票了，就顺便补个评测

review_english: You must put down everything you are doing to experience the invincible Baldur's Gate 3. In a sense, it is the perfect work to save me from the game drought. After experiencing it in the PS4 and PS5 era, many 3A characters belong to the opening characters and I know what is going to happen next. So in recent years, I have played more word games and pixel games made by the Japanese. As an aside, Cell Divine Comedy is actually quite fun. It is also good to just play some performances without watching live games. But occasionally I want to play some interesting works with better graphics. The Witcher 3, which is most suitable for my taste, is too old and has been played n times. Just when the blogger was confused, Bo 3 was released. The graphics and performances are impeccable. The plot is not that interesting but the performance is normal and there is no uncomfortable content at all. It is very perfect in every detail. The only fault that can be picked is that it is a bit anticlimactic like Divinity Original Sin 2. I hope that Larian's next work can continue to reflect his love for games. I purchased the digital version on ps5. Not long ago, I also replenished my ticket on PC, so I’ll post a review by the way.

review: Dnd的集大成之作，我最震惊的点是他把所有可能的选项全部都做出来了，及其自由的玩法让我一开始甚至有点不适应，而且他和巫师三不一样的点在于，你有时候会摇出来大失败，但是因为你有四个人，有时候开动脑筋用两次行动点在一些很关键的时刻拯救了队伍的成就感是无语伦比的！大推荐！！！ 只不过可惜明萨拉被我初见杀  用那个大地精的号角直接把地精营地给屠了，最后还把大地精给嘎了，这个让我有点爽。最爽的一个剧情，最让我集中精力的是逃离拉斐尔的府邸的路上，从头高能到尾，不能长休，只有一个回复泉水，还是唯一有专属BGM的boss。当时光顾着框框至圣斩爽了，打到最后一点蓝没有，硬生生靠着变身把拉斐尔磨死。最后的决战也很夸张，我没有做好准备，耐色脑的机制试了三次才打过。给我留下深刻映像的一个游戏！

review_english: A masterpiece of Dnd. What shocked me the most was that it made all the possible options, and the free gameplay made me a little uncomfortable at first. And the difference between it and the Witcher 3 is that sometimes you will fail. But because you have four people, sometimes you use your brain to use two action points to save the team at some very critical moments. The sense of accomplishment is unparalleled. Highly recommended. It's just a pity that Minsala was killed by me at the first sight. I used the horn of the hobgoblin to directly massacre the goblin camp. In the end, I defeated the Hobgoblin. This made me feel a little happy. The most exciting plot. What made me concentrate the most was on the way to escape from Raphael's mansion. It was very energetic from the beginning to the end. There was no long rest. There was only one recovery spring. It was also the only boss with an exclusive BGM. It was great to focus on the frame of Holy Slash. I didn't have any mana until the last point. I had to transform and wear Raphael to death. The final battle was also exaggerated. I was not prepared. The mechanism of Nese Brain was tried three times before I defeated it. A game that left a deep impression on me

review:
BG3 is frustrating to me because almost every 5e character I've played in the last decade is unplayable in this game. I cannot play my:
- Castlevania inspired, Oath of Vengeance whip paladin
- Cavalier
- Unicorn servant druid, actually a lot of the customization options are really lacking. You've next to no control over your build apart from what race you picked. Customization wise, this game is decades behind. There are a tonne of hairstyles, but once you start looking through them your realize some of them are repeating elements/transposing the back from one to another. Just... let me build my own haircut? ♥♥♥♥.
- Lore Master Wizard (This one was UA, so not super surprising, I really enjoyed playing him though)
- Undying Warlock/Shadow Sorc, while they eventually added shadow sorcery in patch 8, Undying Warlock is still MIA, which sucks cuz it's a really underserved archtype imo, but I played mine as a sort of 'cursed to immortality'
- Grave Domain, this one is fine, they've got sooo many of the cleric domains, and admittedly this character was a bureaucratic nightmare at the table. I entirely wanted it for the level 6 feature of cancelling crits, such a good utility for a healer to have

On top of this, unvoiced protagonists are so hard for me to get into. There's so much voice acting in this game, and the fact you won't hear your character say any of their dialogue is especially frustrating when playing one of the premade characters. If nothing else, I'd expect the fully voiced characters to maintain their voice if I'm playing them. There's a lot more stuff but it's basic frustration in TTRPGs when you have a railroading DM, but it's a video game, so can't be too mad that the playspace isn't infinite. However, reworking the intro for full release to make it so much more confined and rushed feeling was odd. I'd played that intro idk how many times in early access. Some of the other changes from EA>Release make sense. Gale was unbearable, kill on sight levels of unbearable. Astarion wears that crown now. IDC who you are, hold me at knifepoint and we're past potentially being friends. Also, multiclassing being blocked on easy difficulty is just strange. Seriously, seriously strange. To the point I'm concerned about the code of the game.

EDIT: After some contemplation, I decided that even with all that said, they still didn't siphon us financially for DLC so, at least it's a whole game. It's got plenty of flaws, but, you'll get what you pay for without needing to shill out more.

Some of the other changes from EA Release make sense. Gale was unbearable, kill on sight levels of unbearable. Astarion wears that crown now. IDC who you are, hold me at knifepoint and we're past potentially being friends. Also, multiclassing being blocked on easy difficulty is just strange. Seriously, seriously strange. To the point I'm concerned about the code of the game. EDIT After some contemplation, I decided that even with all that said, they still didn't siphon us financially for DLC so, at least it's a whole game. It's got plenty of flaws, but, you'll get what you pay for without needing to shill out more.
review_english: BG3 is frustrating to me because almost every 5e character I've played in the last decade is unplayable in this game. I cannot play my - Castlevania inspired, Oath of Vengeance whip paladin - Cavalier - Unicorn servant druid, actually a lot of the customization options are really lacking. You've next to no control over your build apart from what race you picked. Customization wise, this game is decades behind. There are a tonne of hairstyles, but once you start looking through them your realize some of them are repeating elements transposing the back from one to another. Just... let me build my own haircut? . - Lore Master Wizard (This one was UA, so not super surprising, I really enjoyed playing him though) - Undying Warlock Shadow Sorc, while they eventually added shadow sorcery in patch 8, Undying Warlock is still MIA, which sucks cuz it's a really underserved archtype imo, but I played mine as a sort of 'cursed to immortality' - Grave Domain, this one is fine, they've got sooo many of the cleric domains, and admittedly this character was a bureaucratic nightmare at the table. I entirely wanted it for the level 6 feature of cancelling crits, such a good utility for a healer to have On top of this, unvoiced protagonists are so hard for me to get into. There's so much voice acting in this game, and the fact you won't hear your character say any of their dialogue is especially frustrating when playing one of the premade characters. If nothing else, I'd expect the fully voiced characters to maintain their voice if I'm playing them. To the point I'm concerned about the code of the game. review:
I looooooove this game please play it. The story is peak, the music is peak, character customization is peak, and the combat is amazing. Easily dumped 170+ hours on this game one of my favs oat. 
    
     My favorite part of the game is the dnd turn based combat (Especially in single player) the combat is strategic and the different weaknesses, strengths, and the environment make the player approach a battle differently and utilize their different party members as each class has things it excels at. As well, the spell slots and actions/bonus actions make the player really think about their choices as a spell slot used in one battle can affect you in the next(if you don't long rest).
     
     I also love much one choice/dialogue option can change your entire run and, if you are really upset with your choice, you can always go back a save and approach a situation differently. After your first playthrough, you will want to immediately play again except switching up your race, class, and party setup. I loved how you are able to change the class of companions, allowing for infinite party combinations even in single player. 
    
     Honestly I recommend this game to anyone who loves rpgs, a good story, strategy, or just something you can hyper fixate on for a couple months(like me!). This game is also really fun multiplayer and playing it multiplayer makes it a whole new fun and chaotic experience. I really hope Larian clutches with another turn based rpg type game as I will be buying it immediately. I love you baldurs gate mwah ❤︎❤︎❤︎

review: Wait and buy perhaps when this game goes on deep sale, but its not worth full price, I expected better from high ratings and a steep price in comp. to many other good games on steam. Enough misfires in development for me to say no, despite a strong refresh and infusion of nostalgia from the original games. Progression was just better throughout in the originals, and beautiful graphics & storyline update cannot cover that. Things are not often well explained without searching the internet for direction. I gave it a good go even with complaints about the system, eventually quit when they capped leveling at 12, long before the story ended. Just another in a in a list of poor gameplay progression decisions. It was the final straw, just killed the experience for me and a bit insulting to the player imo. Appreciate why they received an overwhelmingly positive rating, I see the great thought and artwork put in to this game, but I think the rating was largely based on the enthusiasm of bringing back a classic, nothing else. I question Larian's revival of future titles based on this experience.

review:
Prior to this game I had absolutely no background familiarity with Baldur's Gate, or DnD. I casually tried to start a campaign a year ago, but dropped it after about an hour or two because all the mechanics were new to me. I kept reading these glowing reviews about it, so recently I decided to really try to commit to getting past the learning curve so I could give it a proper go.

Six weeks later... where did my life go? All I did was play this game, think about this game, and read about this game for six straight weeks. Like, it definitely crossed the border of being a problem because it was so addictive for me. I still feel gross just thinking about it. So, obviously I would highly recommend it.

review: I have countless hours invested in this game (mostly on my PS5 with over 450+hours) and another 15 on PC. This is genuinely one of the best games I have ever played. The replay value on it is amazing and playing with friends is extremely fun. My favorite thing about BG3 is that every choice feels like it has a purpose and is meaningful. The writing is also fantastic, the voice acting is perfection, and the world of BG3 feels rich in a way that doesn't make sense but feels right. The companions are also incredible and deserve their praise too. They're complex, memorable, and genuine. It isn't hard to like or dislike them as you would your own friends in your own circle. I would recommend this game to anyone and everyone who is into RPG or turn based combat. It's truly one of the best games I've played in a long time and will probably continue to play it until I can't anymore.

review:
One of the best RPGs I've played for a long time, and I've played plenty of them through the years. Engaging characters, tremendous replayability, multiple outcomes, endings, customisation options, and plenty of community mods to optimise your gameplay. 
If you're like me and knew absolutely zilch about the D&D universe, BG3 will gently guide you through it. It's very new player friendly with the easiest difficulty, and I recommend starting on that until you get the hang of spells & game mechanics.
The only 'negatives' I can think of are that the turn based combat can feel slow sometimes, especially when you've done a few play throughs (and you will, lol). Also the lack of WASD movement and the camera can be annoying, but not to the extent where it's difficult to get through the game. There is a mod you can DL for WASD movement if it really is a pain for you.

review:
Parliamo di Baldur's Gate 3. Un gioco così vasto, rifinito e ossessivamente dettagliato da sembrare sviluppato non da esseri umani, ma da una colonia di maghi insonni nutriti esclusivamente con manuali di D&D e disperazione creativa.
Ed è, senza grandi giri di parole, un capolavoro assoluto.

Creazione personaggio: il vero primo boss
Tu pensi: “faccio un personaggio veloce e inizio”.
Tre ore dopo sei ancora lì a discutere con te stesso se:
*fare un tiefling warlock tormentato
*un druido fungino moralmente ambiguo
*un bardo manipolatore con problemi emotivi
*o rifare tutto da capo perché “gli occhi non convincono”
La creazione personaggio in Baldur’s Gate 3 non è un menu. È una spirale psicologica.
Razze, classi, sottoclassi, background, dialoghi, estetica, allineamento implicito. Ogni scelta sembra avere peso reale. E il peggio? Ce l’ha davvero.

Gameplay: libertà pericolosa
Questo gioco ha un approccio semplice: “se ti viene in mente qualcosa di intelligente, probabilmente puoi farlo”.
*Spingi nemici dai dirupi
*Parli invece di combattere
*Avveleni un accampamento
*Ti trasformi in animale per infiltrarti
*Lanci un goblin contro un altro goblin
Il sistema prende le regole di Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition e le traduce in un videogioco con una fedeltà quasi inquietante.
E la cosa assurda è che raramente sembra limitante.

Scrittura: livelli quasi offensivi di qualità
Qui succede qualcosa di raro: i personaggi sembrano persone.
Difetti, traumi, desideri, egoismi, paure. Nessuno è semplicemente “il guerriero” o “la maga”.
E poi c’è il dettaglio devastante: quasi ogni dialogo può cambiare qualcosa.
Non stai scegliendo tra:
*risposta buona
*risposta cattiva
Stai navigando sistemi emotivi complessi che reagiscono davvero a quello che fai.

Esplorazione: densità biologica
Ogni area è viva.
Apri una porta casuale e trovi:
*una quest secondaria enorme
*un culto segreto
*un NPC memorabile
*una tragedia nascosta
*oppure un pollo infernale senziente, perché evidentemente qualcuno in Larian aveva troppo tempo libero
La quantità di contenuto non è solo enorme. È stratificata. Densa. Quasi patologicamente curata.

Combattimento: caos tattico controllato
A turni, ma dinamico.
Altezza, fuoco, ghiaccio, elettricità, posizione, sinergie. Il campo di battaglia è un ecosistema interattivo.
E ogni combattimento può degenerare magnificamente:
*barili che esplodono
*evocazioni fuori controllo
*alleati che precipitano nel vuoto
*piani perfetti distrutti da un dado crudele
Perché sì: i dadi decidono ancora. E a volte ti odiano personalmente.

Il vero problema: il tempo smette di esistere
Questo gioco altera la percezione temporale.
Dici: “faccio una quest veloce”.
Poi:
*parli con un NPC
*entri in una caverna
*trovi un complotto
*combatti tre ore
*inizi una romance discutibile
*cambi build
*riapri il creator mentalmente
E improvvisamente sono le 2:43 del mattino e stai rivalutando le tue priorità esistenziali.

Difetti (pochi, ma esistono)
*Atto 3 meno pulito rispetto al resto
*Complessità che può intimidire
*Alcuni bug inevitabili vista la scala mostruosa
*Rischio concreto di perdere la vita sociale

Sintesi finale
Baldur’s Gate 3 è uno dei migliori RPG mai realizzati.
Non perché faccia una cosa perfettamente. Ma perché ne fa cento a un livello assurdo:
*scrittura
*libertà
*roleplay
*combattimento
*esplorazione
*reattività del mondo
È il raro caso in cui l’ambizione gigantesca non collassa sotto il proprio peso.
E quando finisci una campagna, il pensiero immediato non è: “che bel finale”.
È: “ok… ma se facessi un drow necromante completamente instabile?”
E il ciclo ricomincia.

review_english: Let's talk about Baldur's Gate 3. A game so vast, refined and obsessively detailed that it seems developed not by humans, but by a colony of sleepless wizards fed exclusively on D D manuals and creative desperation. And it is, without mincing words, an absolute masterpiece. Character creation the real first boss You think I'll make a quick character and start. Three hours later you're still there debating with yourself whether to make a tormented tiefling warlock a morally ambiguous fungal druid a manipulative bard with emotional problems or do it all over again because the eyes don't convince Character creation in Baldur's Gate 3 is not a menu. It's a psychological spiral. Races, classes, subclasses, background, dialogue, aesthetics, implicit alignment. Every choice seems to have real weight. And the worst? He really has it. Dangerous Freedom Gameplay This game has a simple approach if you think of something clever, you can probably do it. You push enemies off cliffs You talk instead of fighting You poison a camp You transform into an animal to infiltrate You throw a goblin at another goblin The system takes the rules of Dungeons Dragons 5th Edition and translates them into a video game with an almost uncanny fidelity. And the crazy thing is that it rarely feels limiting. Writing almost offensive levels of quality Something rare happens here: the characters seem like people. Defects, traumas, desires, selfishness, fears. No one is simply the warrior or the sorceress. And then there's the devastating detail: almost every dialogue can change something. You're not choosing between good answer bad answer You're navigating complex emotional systems that actually react to what you do. Biological density exploration Every area is alive. You open a random door and find a huge side quest, a secret cult, a memorable NPC, a hidden tragedy, or a sentient hell chicken, because apparently someone in Larian had too much free time The amount of content isn't just huge. It's layered. Dense. Almost pathologically cured. Controlled tactical chaos combat Turn-based, yet dynamic. Height, fire, ice, electricity, position, synergies. The battlefield is an interactive ecosystem. And every fight can degenerate magnificently, barrels exploding, evocations out of control, allies falling into the void, perfect plans destroyed by a cruel dice. Because yes, the dice still decide. And sometimes they hate you personally. The real problem: time ceases to exist. This game alters time perception. You say I'll do a quick quest. Then you talk to an NPC you enter a cave you find a plot you fight for three hours you start a questionable romance you change builds you reopen the creator mentally And suddenly it's 2.43 in the morning and you're reevaluating your existential priorities. Defects (few, but they exist) Act 3 less clean than the rest Complexity that can intimidate Some unavoidable bugs given the monstrous scale Real risk of losing your social life Final summary Baldur's Gate 3 is one of the best RPGs ever made. Not because it does something perfectly. But why does it make a hundred to an absurd level writing freedom roleplay combat exploration reactivity of the world It is the rare case in which gigantic ambition does not collapse under its own weight. And when you finish a campaign, the immediate thought is nothing but a good ending. That's ok but what if I made a completely unstable drow necromancer? And the cycle starts again.

review:
Baldur’s Gate 3 is one of the best RPGs I have ever played, and after nearly 300 hours, I can confidently say it deserves the praise it gets. It combines strong storytelling, tactical turn-based combat, meaningful exploration, and incredible artistic design into one massive experience. While it has a few flaws, they are nowhere near enough to take away from what makes the game special.

The story and roleplaying are easily some of the game’s greatest strengths. The choices you make feel meaningful, whether they affect your companions, quests, or the larger world around you. The characters are well-written, memorable, and full of personality, making it easy to become invested in their stories. Even smaller side quests often feel important because of the writing and the way the game reacts to your decisions.

The turn-based combat is another major highlight. It rewards strategy, creativity, and experimentation rather than just rushing into every fight. Positioning, spell choices, environmental effects, and party builds all matter, which makes battles feel satisfying and unique. The combat can be challenging, especially for players unfamiliar with Dungeons & Dragons mechanics, but learning the system is part of what makes the game so rewarding.

Exploration, world-building, and presentation are also outstanding. Every area feels carefully designed, filled with secrets, hidden paths, interesting characters, and lore that makes the world feel alive. The art direction, environments, music, voice acting, and character animations all add to the immersion. The game does have some technical issues, especially in later parts like Act 3, and it can occasionally feel overwhelming because of its size and complexity, but these problems are small compared to everything the game does right.

Overall, Baldur’s Gate 3 is a massive, detailed, and unforgettable RPG that sets a very high standard for storytelling and player freedom. Its few negatives, such as bugs, performance dips, or a steep learning curve, are not enough to stop me from recommending it. If you enjoy deep stories, tactical combat, meaningful choices, and a world that rewards curiosity, Baldur’s Gate 3 is absolutely worth playing.

review:
Larian did a superb job of adapting 5th ed dungeons and dragons to their own engine. I firmly believe that this may encourage a generation of new players for the table top roleplaying game. (With odd quirks like obsessions with throwing people  and barrels.)
My one gripe is the outlandish backstories of playable NPC's. This is a known problem for us veteran table top players. Having a player turn up with a first level character with a backstory describing a mighty hero is a no no, and will inevitably lead to frustration. Gale's thread is amongst the most egregious starting character backgrounds I have ever come across.

review:
Act 1 is some of the best RPG design I've seen — tight, interconnected, every corner has something going on. I've been through it roughly 10 times. Act 2 maybe 2-3. Act 3 exactly once — and only because I forced myself to stop restarting and actually finish the damn game. That ratio tells you everything about where the polish lives.

What does need to be said: this is not Baldur's Gate. I've played BG1, BG2, and Pillars of Eternity. This is Divinity: Original Sin 3 wearing the name. That's not a complaint — Divinity Original Sin 2 is excellent, and so is this. But if you're coming in expecting the spiritual heir to BG2, recalibrate.

I could probably nitpick something if I really tried — but the fact that I have to try that hard tells you everything you need to know.

Recommended.

一周目完结写测评
很惊艳，RPG居然能做到这个程度
剧情内容非常丰富，选择支很多，玩家的选择可以大幅改变队友和NPC的命运。队友的各种结局也没有绝对的好坏，纯看个人选择
探索和玩法的自由度非常高，如果用少玩5毛钱的梗来说，那玩这游戏就是倒赚300块
战斗也很有意思，比较独特的回合制玩法，之前没玩过类似的，但机制比较复杂，没有相关作品经验的不看攻略很难顶
解谜很多地方也得看攻略，有些东西很难自己想出来
多周目的游玩价值非常高，战斗上可以尝试各种流派，剧情上可以选不同分支。准备之后再开二周目慢慢探细节
----------------------

review_english: I’m writing a review after finishing one game. It’s amazing that an RPG can achieve this level. The plot content is very rich. There are many choices. Players’ choices can greatly change the fate of teammates and NPCs. The various endings of teammates are not absolutely good or bad. It purely depends on personal choice. The degree of freedom in exploration and gameplay is very high. If you use the joke of playing 5 cents less, you will make 300 yuan by playing this game. The combat is also very interesting. It has a relatively unique turn-based gameplay. I have never played anything similar before, but the mechanism is relatively complicated. If you have no experience in related works, it will be difficult to read the guide. There are many places where you need to read the guide to solve puzzles. Some things are difficult to figure out on your own. The play value of multiple weeks is very high. You can try various genres in combat. You can choose different branches in the plot. After preparation, start the second week to slowly explore the details. --------------------------- Thank you to the friend who gave me the game. I just added it to the wish list and considered buying it. I received the gift.

review:
好游戏，但玩不下去是我的问题
如果我今年12岁，我可能会仔细去了解DND龙与地下城的游戏设定背景故事，然后去仔细学习CRPG的游戏策略再来玩这个。但尼玛我今年都22岁了，9岁的时候就玩魔兽争霸3这种即时战略游戏，让我突然玩这种回合制游戏堪比坐牢。这个游戏的背景故事和暴雪的魔兽世界相似，所以什么精灵、矮人、兽人种族设定都能接受，如果不做成回合制，那很像魔兽世界的游戏玩法，大量的文本和任务我是没有什么问题的，只是这个回合制设定我一直操作加上推图，我这个年龄属于是山猪吃不了细糠了。我习惯于要么像大战场策略运营作战，要么英雄主义单兵一路打怪升级。像炉石传说这种回合制的卡牌游戏，就是单纯的有那么一套设定在里面，卡牌在那边。你看剧情的同时每局给你的卡牌是不一样的，但你选的是一个角色或者一个背景的人
这游戏上来一堆种族职业，没问题，但四个人团本，每回合操作，我要记很多技能和技巧，同时还要去看剧情做任务，要不是看了攻略还不知道长休会扣时间。总之，我没这个精力同时做这么多事了
希望等到下一次再打开这个游戏的时候，我状态好一些或者有人和我一起玩吧，唉

review_english: It’s a good game, but it’s my problem that I can’t play it. If I were 12 years old, I would probably carefully understand the background story of DND Dungeons and Dragons, and then carefully study the game strategies of CRPG before playing this game. But damn, I’m 22 years old this year. I played real-time strategy games like Warcraft 3 when I was 9 years old. Suddenly playing this kind of turn-based game is like going to jail. The background story of this game is similar to Blizzard’s World of Warcraft, so any race setting of elves, dwarves, orcs is acceptable. If it is not made turn-based, it will be very similar to the gameplay of World of Warcraft. I have no problem with a lot of texts and tasks. It’s just that I have been operating and pushing the turn-based setting. At this age, the wild boar can’t eat fine chaff. I am used to either strategically operating operations in large battlefields or heroic individual soldiers fighting monsters and upgrading. Turn-based card games like Hearthstone simply have such a set of settings in it. The cards are there. When you watch the plot, the cards given to you in each game are different. But you choose a character or a person with a background. There are a lot of racial professions in this game, which is no problem. But in the four-person group version, I have to memorize a lot of skills and techniques for each round. At the same time, I have to read the plot and do tasks. If I hadn’t read the guide, I don’t know that long breaks will deduct time. Anyway, I don’t have the energy to do so many things at the same time. I hope that when I open the game again next time, I will be in a better state or someone will play with me. Alas.

review: This is the best game I have ever played hands down. The richness and depth of the content and characters in mind blowing. Fantastic graphics, epic storyline, badass character customization, phenomenal dialogue paired with top notch voice acting, crazy options for multiplayer, and unbelievable replay value are just some of the amazing things you will find in this game. It was even more impressive than the stockpile of 10s the reviews indicate. I just beat it for the first time and WOW. WOW. WOW. WOW. Ready for playthrough number 2.
